entry.title: Re: Custom Cube for Margin Analysis - Calculated Key Figure shows no data
entry.content:
If the calculated measure shows values in ADT data preview but not in Analysis Office or other BW tools, the issue is likely in the BI semantics layer (exception aggregation, currency handling, or query variables).

If the calculated measure is already blank in ADT preview, then the issue is in the CDS modeling. In that case, verify aggregation, currency semantics, and whether the measure is properly propagated when one cube consumes another. Calculated measures defined in a lower cube are not automatically exposed or re-annotated in higher-level cubes.  

Thanks, 

Koushik G

entry.title: SAP IoT Edge to SAP CPI Integration – Enterprise Architecture Explained
entry.content:
In today’s industrial landscape, organizations are rapidly adopting IoT-enabled solutions to collect real-time data from machines, sensors, and PLCs. However, collecting device data is only the first step — the real value comes from integrating this data into enterprise systems like ERP, analytics platforms, and business applications.

This is where SAP IoT Edge, SAP IoT, and SAP Cloud Platform Integration work together to create a scalable, secure, and enterprise-ready IoT architecture.

What is SAP IoT Edge ?
1) It is a Edge-side software installed near machines (factory / plant / gateway).
2) Collects data from Sensors, PLCs, and industrial machines.
3) Supports OPC-UA, MQTT, Modbus, REST Adapters.
4) Performs local filtering, aggregation, buffering.
5) Works with low-latency and unstable internet.

SAP IoT Edge – Role in Integration
1) Direct interface to physical devices.
2) Handles industrial protocols not supported by CPI.
3) Reduces data volume before cloud transmission.
4) Ensures reliability and near real-time processing.

What is SAP IoT (BTP Services)?

1) SAP IOT BTP is a Cloud-based IoT services running on SAP BTP.
2) Central IoT platform for device onboarding and management.
3) Provides rules, events and APIs.
4) Acts as a secure IoT backbone for SAP landscape.

SAP IoT (BTP) – Role in Integration

1) Manages device lifecycle and security.
2) Processes and enriches IoT data in the cloud.
3) Triggers rules and events.
4) Exposes data via APIs/events for SAP CPI consumption.

What is SAP CPI?

1) SAP Cloud Platform Integration is a middleware.
2) Integrates SAP and non-SAP applications.
3) Enterprise-grade integration, monitoring, and governance.
4) Does NOT communicate directly with sensors or PLCs.

SAP CPI – Role in Integration

1) Consumes APIs/events from SAP IoT (BTP).
2) Performs mapping, routing, validation, enrichment.
3) Handles error handling, retries, and monitoring.
4) Integrates with S/4HANA, ECC, Analytics, Non-SAP apps.

--> Now we will see the what are the usable adapters for the all three integration aspects .

Usable Adapters – SAP IoT Edge

1) OPC-UA (Open Platform Communications Unified Architecture) –> Industrial machines and PLCs.
2) MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport ) –> Lightweight device messaging.
3) Modbus –> Legacy industrial devices.
4) REST / HTTP –> Smart devices and gateways.

Usable Adapters – SAP IoT (BTP)

MQTT –> Secure device data ingestion
REST / HTTP – > API-based access to IoT data
Event APIs – > Event-driven processing and notifications

Usable Adapters – SAP CPI

HTTP / REST –> Primary IoT integration adapter
OData –> SAP S/4HANA APIs
SOAP –> Legacy web services
RFC / IDoc –> SAP ECC / On-Prem integration

Recommended Architecture (Enterprise Ready/High Volume)

-->ow we going through the Architecture of the integration between SAP IOT EDGE and SAP CPI.

Architecture -1

 Alternative Architecture (PoC / Low Volume)

-->Now is the alternative for the above , it will be used only for the POC's point of view like for the low volumes of data from the IOT devices.

Architecture -2

--> CPI is cloud-only and not suitable for high-volume, real-time device data.
--> It also lacks device management capabilities.
--> Direct integration should be limited to PoC or very low-volume scenarios.

IFLOW DEMO

DEMO IFLOW

 -->So, Now we are having a iflow demo here.

-->In this demo I have taken the postman to post the json data with temperatures and first it will convert the json data to xml data.

--> After the conversion, the xml data is routed based on the conditions we provided like when the temperatures are greater than or equal to  90 degrees Centigrade it will be moved to content modifier and the data is modified for the mail payload and sent the alert to the team.(In this I haven't connected mail).

-->When normal temperatures comes , then the data will be moved to default route.

Key Takeaways

1) IoT Edge  = Near the machine.
2) SAP IoT (BTP)  = Brain in the cloud.
3) SAP CPI = Messenger to business systems.
4) Using SAP IoT (BTP) in between makes the solution more stable, secure, and enterprise-ready.

Conclusion

A robust IoT integration landscape requires clear separation of responsibilities across layers. SAP IoT Edge manages device-level communication and industrial protocols near the machines, SAP Internet of Things on SAP BTP handles cloud processing, device management, and event exposure, and SAP Cloud Platform Integration integrates IoT data into enterprise systems like SAP S/4HANA.

For enterprise and high-volume scenarios, using SAP IoT (BTP) between IoT Edge and CPI ensures scalability, security, and stability. Direct integration should be reserved for PoC or low-volume use cases.

entry.title: Re: AND function in SAC is not working
entry.content:
@Aliyah_Mazaya67 

With limited dimensionality information of your model, Your condition test looks fine so I do not think there is an issue with AND conditions. What I can see is all you conditions when TRUE they write to the same intersection.

 

DATA([d/Measures] = "VALIDATION",[d/DIM_MANPOWER_BUDGET_ITEM] = "A5000_205",[d/DIM_CURRENCY] = "#",[d/Date] =[d/Version].[p/M1Y1]) = 1
 
DATA([d/Measures] = "VALIDATION",[d/DIM_MANPOWER_BUDGET_ITEM] = "A5000_205",[d/DIM_CURRENCY] = "#",[d/Date] =[d/Version].[p/M1Y1]) = 2
 
DATA([d/Measures] = "VALIDATION",[d/DIM_MANPOWER_BUDGET_ITEM] = "A5000_205",[d/DIM_CURRENCY] = "#",[d/Date] =[d/Version].[p/M1Y1]) = 3
 
DATA([d/Measures] = "VALIDATION",[d/DIM_MANPOWER_BUDGET_ITEM] = "A5000_205",[d/DIM_CURRENCY] = "#",[d/Date] =[d/Version].[p/M1Y1]) = 4
 
 
Unless there is another dimension which differentiates over the test conditions like EMPLOYEE or EMPLOYEE group  you Validations will overwrite each other. The reason you see only 4 is due the reason that it is the last DATA() statement in order of writing and the result overwrites all previous results as the intersection is same.
 
Hope this helps !
Nikhil

entry.title: 🤖⚡ Meet Traceability Agent, my first custom Joule Agent for SAP Digital Manufacturing. ⚡🤖
entry.content:
Introduction

This is not a dashboard.
This is not a report.
This is an AI Agent that understands your manufacturing history instantly.

Traceability Agent is an expert in as-built and batch traceability, capable of answering complex questions in seconds:

🔎Top-down: What went into this SFC, batch, or assembly?
🔁Bottom-up: Where was this batch, material, or component used?
⚡Instant analysis across orders, SFCs, batches, and inventory
🧠 No manual investigation. No digging. Just ask.

What used to take minutes or hours… now takes seconds.
This is a massive leap toward AI-driven manufacturing intelligence, where Joule Agents become true digital experts embedded directly into SAP Digital Manufacturing.

What are Joule Agents?

Joule Agents are specialized, generative AI-powered, and collaborative agents from SAP designed to automate complex, non-deterministic business workflows across finance, supply chain, procurement, and HR. They function as intelligent, context-aware teammates that use SAP Knowledge Graph and business data to proactively resolve multi-step tasks, interact with systems, and make decisions.

Learn more: Joule Agents: How SAP Uniquely Delivers AI Agents That Truly Mean Business
Demo: Joule Agents: Dispute Resolution Agent

Key aspects of Joule agents include:

  • Context-Aware Automation: Unlike generic AI, Joule agents are deeply integrated with SAP data, understanding how actions in one area, such as the supply chain, affect others, like finance.
  • Proactive Action: These agents can operate independently in the background, identifying exceptions, sending alerts, and preparing recommendations without constant user prompting.
  • Collaboration: Joule agents can work together, with some agents calling upon others to solve, for example, a complex, multi-functional project that requires both procurement and logistics expertise.
  • Customization: Using Joule Studio's agent builder, companies can build, test, and deploy their own custom agents to meet specific needs if pre-built options are unavailable.
  • Use Cases: Examples include the ExpenseIt Agent for analyzing receipts, Career and Talent Development Agents for HR planning, and Meeting Location Planner Agents in SAP Concur.

Joule agents are accessed through conversational interfaces, acting as an extension of the broader Joule AI assistant.

Learn more about Joule Studio, Agent Builder, and take Joule Studio Product Tour.

How I built Traceability Agent?

Joule Agents can easily leverage Joule Skills out-of-the-box as building blocks (Tools) to retrieve data and perform actions. However, for Traceability Agent, I wanted to try something new. For the first time, I wanted to explore MCP Servers and how that could enhance or improve not only my overall build flow, but also the overall outcome of the agent.

So, I ended up with the following architecture:

Where I deployed a MCP Server in SAP BTP:

That connects to SAP Digital Manufacturing HANA Database directly:

Which gives the access my Joule Agent would need to retrieve SAP Digital Manufacturing data.

With that in place, I was able to start building agents using Joule Studio.

First, defining Name, Description, Expertise and Instructions:

Then, defining my preferred LLM Model Settings, which in my case I used Google Gemini 2.5 Pro:

Then, adding the MCP Server mapping:

And adjusting my output preferences:

So I could then Test it, Release it and Deploy it.

So what!? What this Agent is capable of?

Glad you asked! 🙂 This agent can do really cool as-built and traceability analysis, including:

🔎Top-down: What went into this SFC, batch, or assembly?

🔁Bottom-up: Where was this batch, material, or component used?

🔝Top and Ranking Batches

and much more!

Now, I´m working on Nonconformance, Rework, OEE and Labor Agents, so stay tuned.

Conclusion

Traceability Agent shows how manufacturing intelligence is evolving from manual investigation to instant, conversational insight.

What once required navigating multiple screens and reports can now be answered in seconds by an AI Agent that understands your SAP Digital Manufacturing data and context. By combining Joule Agents, Joule Studio, and MCP Servers, this approach enables AI-powered experts that can analyze traceability, identify relationships, and deliver answers on demand.

 

What do you think about custom Joule Agents for SAP Digital Manufacturing? Let me know in the comments!

Stay tuned for more! Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Inspection Lot Quantity field's value is not printing in Quality Certificate
entry.content:
We have developed a Quality Certificate for batches and mapped the Inspection Lot Quantity field using binding. Previously, the field value was printed successfully. However, for the past 3 to 4 months, we have observed that the Inspection Lot Quantity field value is no longer printing in the Quality Certificate for batches.

A similar issue occurred with the Inspection Date field. Therefore, we created a support case with SAP. After analyzing the Quality Certificate form, SAP proposed a solution to correct the binding and add the field logic in BADI QM_QCERTIFICATE_OUT_MODIFY.

After implementing the suggested changes, the Inspection Date field is now printing successfully. However, the Inspection Lot Quantity field value is still not printing.

We have tried multiple combinations and approaches, but we are still unable to print the Inspection Lot Quantity field value.

Could anyone please help us resolve this issue?

entry.title: Communication Arrangements in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Communication Arrangements

Communication Arrangements is one of the Key User Extensibility Tools.

With Communication Arrangements App,

  • Communication Arrangements details can be viewed.
  • Custom Communication Arrangements can be created with standard communication or custom communication scenarios using Communication Arrangements App.

Finding the Standard Communication Scenarios in SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Navigate to SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Choose the Product

SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition

Navigate to APIs -> ODATA V2 / ODATA V4.

Search Required API

List of results for Purchase Order.

Purchase Order API Overview

API Resources

Navigate to API Resources -> Attributes -> Communication Scenario

API Specifications

Authentication Methods for API

Configuration Details for API

Extensibility

Custom Fields can be added to the API using below Business Contexts - Header and Items in Custom Fields App with Key User Extensibility.

API Reference

List of Entity Sets Available in the APIs.

Communication Scenarios

Navigate to Display Communication Scenarios,

All these are Standard Communication Scenarios.

Standard Communication Scenarios of Purchase Order - SAP_COM_0053

Steps to Create Communication Arrangements

Maintain Communication Users

Navigate to Maintain Communication Users App.

Maintain Communication Users is to create Communication Users for the API Access.

Choose New button to create Communication User

Add User Name and Password.

Propose Password can be chosen to generate password by system.

Save the Communication User.

If communication User is added to any Communication system or communication Arrangements, here it shows those details.

Communication System

Navigate to Communication System App.

Communication System is to maintain communication system with host name and communication User.

Provide Communication System ID and Name.

Add the Host Name

Add necessary details

Choose Add button to add Inbound Communication User.

Available Authentication Methods

Required details for SSL Client Certificate.

Required details for OAuth 2.0

Choose the Authentication Method, and choose User Name/Client ID.

Click on Ok after choosing Communication User.

Communication User is added.

 Communication System is saved.

Communication Arrangements

Navigate to Communication Arrangements app.

Communication Arrangements is to generate Inbound Service URLs by adding Communication System and Communication User.

Choose New Option to create Custom Communication Arrangements.

Standard or Custom Communication Scenarios is required to create Custom Communication Arrangements.

Search for the Standard or Custom Communication Scenario

‘SAP_COM_0053’ is the Standard Communication Scenario for the Purchase Order API.

Arrangement appears automatically based on Communication Scenario and it can be changed.

Click on Create button to create Communication Arrangement.

List of Inbound Services available for that Communication Scenario.

WSDL/Service Metadata will be generated once Saved the Communication Arrangement.

Communication System and Communication User is required to create Communication Arrangements.

WSDL/Service Metadata is generated.

Service Metadata

Service URL is available in Communication Arrangement.

Access the Service URLs through the Communication User details.

Conclusion

standard communication or custom communication scenarios are used to create Communication Arrangements.

In Communication Arrangements, OData URLs are generated based on inbound Service.

References

  • Communication Arrangements | SAP Help Portal
  • Maintain Communication Users | SAP Help Portal
  • Communication Systems | SAP Help Portal
  • Integration | SAP Help Portal
  • Extend and Integrate Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Enforce whole numbers only for Quantity in Unit of en...
entry.content:
Good day @diegodelhierro 

Thank you for your question.

When you set decimal place to zero and decimal rounding in the unit of measure setup mainly affects how the unit is defined and displayed but it does not always enforce a hard input validation in every field the way you expect. This quantity entry behaviour is also described in KBA 3631618.

To answer your question, the Public Cloud does not provide a simple standard switch to force the Goods Receipt “Quantity to Unit of Entry” field to accept only whole number for all cases just by changing UoM display or rounding settings.

You may consider the following checks in your system:

  • Open a PO item, confirm which Order Unit your system uses (e.g., PC, EA, ST). The GR app validates against this unit behaviour.

  • In your CBC/configuration for Units of Measure, confirm the UoM is maintained with the same behaviour you expect. Note that this may still not fully block decimals in every GR app scenario.
  • You may create a test PO and then test with a standard integer unit like EA/PC. Post GR and check whether decimals are still accepted. If they are you may consider raising and incident with the supporting details.

A best practice recommendation is, if your business must strictly prevent decimal GR quantities, design your process to ensure relevant materials use an integer UoM in procurement and inform the users.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: End-to-End Automatic Order to Billing Process in SAP SD (Low-Coding Approach)
entry.content:
In line with the customer requirement, an automatic process was designed covering delivery creation, goods issue posting, and billing immediately after sales order creation.

The solution was implemented based entirely on standard SAP SD principles and designed under a low-coding approach, minimizing custom developments while preserving full control over the process.

1. Excluding Specific Customers from the Automatic Process

During the analysis phase, it became necessary to exclude certain customers from the automated flow.

To achieve this:

  • A custom indicator field was defined under Business Partner – SD (FLCU01) view.
  • Using transaction CMOD, field KDGRP4 was renamed as “Automatic Billing Exclusion”.
  • For customers that must remain outside the automated process, value “X” is maintained via customizing.

This design ensures flexibility and allows business users to control participation in the automated process without additional development.

2. Sales Document Configuration (VOV8)

Within VOV8 sales document type configuration, the relevant document types were adjusted by activating the Immediate Delivery setting.

This configuration enables the system to automatically trigger delivery creation directly from the sales order.

3. Dynamic Control in MV45AFZZ (SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE)

At enhancement point MV45AFZZ – SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE, a class-based method was implemented instead of writing logic directly into the include.

The method dynamically reads Business Partner data and manipulates field TVAK-LISOF depending on defined conditions.

Key Logic Summary:

  • If the document creator is not the workflow user (SAP_WFRT), automation is cancelled.
  • KNVV-KVGR4 is checked for the exclusion flag.
  • If the customer is marked with “X”, the automation flag (TVAK-LISOF) is cleared dynamically.
  • Otherwise, the process continues automatically.

This approach ensures that:

  • Standard configuration remains intact.
  • Automation can be centrally controlled.
  • Sales document level settings can be programmatically overridden when necessary.

  

METHOD zsd_save_document_prepare_1.

* inserted by serdar ulgen. 19.01.2026 11:01:55 .

  i_knvv-kunnr = i_vbak-kunnr.
  i_knvv-vkorg = i_vbak-vkorg.
  i_knvv-vtweg = i_vbak-vtweg.
  i_knvv-spart = i_vbak-spart.

  FIELD-SYMBOLS <lv_lisof> TYPE any.
  ASSIGN ('(SAPMV45A)TVAK-LISOF') TO <lv_lisof>.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  IF i_vbak-ernam <> 'SAP_WFRT'.
    CLEAR <lv_lisof>.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  SELECT SINGLE kvgr4
    FROM knvv
    INTO @i_knvv-kvgr4
    WHERE kunnr = @i_knvv-kunnr
      AND vkorg = @i_knvv-vkorg
      AND vtweg = @i_knvv-vtweg
      AND spart = @i_knvv-spart.

  IF i_knvv-kvgr4 = 'X' AND <lv_lisof> = 'A'.
    CLEAR <lv_lisof>.
  ENDIF.

* if u any question please contact with me.

 

4. Automating Goods Issue and Billing

After completing the Sales → Delivery flow, the next phase covered:

  • Goods Issue posting
  • Billing creation

Alternative Approach (Not Preferred)

An alternative solution could involve sequential BAPI calls for goods issue and billing within a custom program.

Although technically feasible, this method requires careful handling of:

  • ENQUEUE / DEQUEUE locks
  • Document synchronization
  • LUW management

Improper lock handling may lead to document conflicts.

 5. Leveraging Standard Output Type REA0

In this implementation, we leveraged the fact that standard SAP output type REA0 can directly trigger billing.

The approach:

  1. The standard program used in REA0 was copied.
  2. A Goods Issue BAPI call was added at the beginning.
  3. Billing creation was kept as standard.
  4. Output determination was assigned correctly to the relevant delivery types using VV21.

This step is critical — without correct output assignment, the automation will not execute.

6. Technical Highlights

  • Goods issue is triggered via WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE_2
  • Billing is created via /SPE/CREATE_NEW_BILLING_CALL
  • Error handling is managed through NAST protocol updates
  • Separate LUW handling is ensured via /SPE/CALL_PROC_IN_NEW_LUW
  • Minimal custom coding
  • Maximum reuse of SAP standard structures
REPORT  zsd_***_****.

TABLES: nast.
CONSTANTS: c_allready_billed TYPE fkstk VALUE 'C'.
DATA: gs_nast            TYPE nast,
      lt_delivery_data   TYPE leshp_delivery_t,
      ls_delivery_data   TYPE LINE OF leshp_delivery_t,
      lt_delivery_items  TYPE leshp_delivery_item_t,
      ls_doc_pos_ver_req TYPE /spe/doc_pos_ver_req,
      lt_doc_pos_ver_req TYPE /spe/doc_pos_ver_req_t,
      lv_dummy_message   TYPE bapiret2-message,
      lt_bapiret2        TYPE bapiret2_t,
      ls_bapiret2        TYPE bapiret2,
      ls_leshp_data      TYPE leshp_data.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <ls_billing_req> TYPE LINE OF leshp_delivery_item_t.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Form  ENTRY
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       create new bill
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM entry USING returncode  TYPE sy-subrc
                 use_screen  TYPE c.

  DATA: ls_vbeln_vl TYPE vbeln_vl,
        lv_leave    TYPE c,                                 "n_1238590
        lv_error    TYPE c,
        ls_vbkok    TYPE vbkok,
        lt_prot     TYPE TABLE OF prott,
        lv_text     TYPE t100-text,
        lv_delivery TYPE vbeln_vl.

  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <ls_return> TYPE bapiret2.

  IF sy-uname = 'SAP_WFRT'.

  lv_delivery = nast-objky.
  ls_vbkok-vbeln_vl  = lv_delivery.
  ls_vbkok-wadat_ist = sy-datum.
  ls_vbkok-wabuc     = abap_true.

  CALL FUNCTION 'WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE_2'
    EXPORTING
      synchron      = abap_true
      vbkok_wa      = ls_vbkok
      delivery      = lv_delivery
    TABLES
      prot          = lt_prot
    EXCEPTIONS
      error_message = 1
      OTHERS        = 2.

  LOOP AT lt_prot ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<error>).
    IF <error>-msgty = 'E' OR <error>-msgty = 'A'.

      SELECT SINGLE text FROM t100 INTO @LV_text WHERE sprsl = @SY-langu AND arbgb = @<error>-msgid AND msgnr = @<error>-msgno.

      IF sy-subrc = 0 AND lv_text IS NOT INITIAL.
        MESSAGE lv_text TYPE 'E'.
      ELSE.
        MESSAGE ID <error>-msgid TYPE 'E' NUMBER <error>-msgno.
      ENDIF.

      returncode = 1.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

ENDIF.


* Redirect this NAST processing to separate LUW - in case it is
* called with dispatching time 4 (immediate processing)
* during update task                                        "v_n_1238590
CALL FUNCTION '/SPE/CALL_PROC_IN_NEW_LUW'
  EXPORTING
    is_nast  = nast
  IMPORTING
    ef_leave = lv_leave.
IF NOT lv_leave IS INITIAL.
*   Per default we set the status on error, so that it can be reprocessed
*   in error processing (RSNAST0F) if there is any syntax error / update
*   termination / ... in the decoupled NAST processing.
  returncode = 4.
  RETURN.
ENDIF.                                                    "^_n_1238590

returncode = 0.
* Get Item Data of Delivery
*  ls_leshp_data-item = 'X'.                                  "n_1822545
* Get Status of Outbound Delivery
ls_leshp_data-head_status = 'X'.
* Copy Nast Entry
gs_nast = nast.
ls_vbeln_vl = nast-objky.

CALL FUNCTION 'LE_DELIVERY_GET_BUFFERED'
  EXPORTING
    if_vbeln               = ls_vbeln_vl
    is_data                = ls_leshp_data
  CHANGING
    cx_deliveries          = lt_delivery_data
  EXCEPTIONS
    no_item_selected       = 1
    selected_item_enqueued = 2
    no_key_specified       = 3
    OTHERS                 = 4.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  MESSAGE e267(/spe/vl) WITH ls_vbeln_vl INTO lv_dummy_message.
  returncode = sy-subrc.
ELSE.

* only one delivery can be found by premisses (VBELN).
  READ TABLE lt_delivery_data INDEX 1 INTO ls_delivery_data.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    IF ls_delivery_data-head_status-fkstk = c_allready_billed.
* Allready billed.
      returncode = 0.
      MESSAGE i265(/spe/vl) WITH ls_vbeln_vl INTO lv_dummy_message.
    ELSE.
*        lt_delivery_items = ls_delivery_data-item.         "v_n_1822545
*        LOOP AT lt_delivery_items ASSIGNING <ls_billing_req>.
*          MOVE-CORRESPONDING <ls_billing_req> TO ls_doc_pos_ver_req.
*          APPEND ls_doc_pos_ver_req TO lt_doc_pos_ver_req.
*        ENDLOOP.
      ls_doc_pos_ver_req-vbeln = ls_delivery_data-head-vbeln.
      APPEND ls_doc_pos_ver_req TO lt_doc_pos_ver_req.    "^_n_1822545

      CALL FUNCTION '/SPE/CREATE_NEW_BILLING_CALL'
        EXPORTING
          it_billing_req     = lt_doc_pos_ver_req
          iv_version_checked = 'X'
        IMPORTING
          et_return          = lt_bapiret2.
      LOOP AT lt_bapiret2 ASSIGNING <ls_return>.
        IF <ls_return>-type = 'E'.
          lv_error = 'X'.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
      IF lv_error = 'X'.
        returncode = 1.
        use_screen = ' '.
      ELSE.
        returncode = 0.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

  ELSE.
* No Entry found !!!
    MESSAGE e267(/spe/vl) WITH ls_vbeln_vl INTO lv_dummy_message.
    returncode = 9.
  ENDIF.
ENDIF.
CALL FUNCTION 'NAST_PROTOCOL_INITIALIZE'.
LOOP AT lt_bapiret2 ASSIGNING <ls_return>.
  IF <ls_return>-type = 'E'.
    lv_error = 'X'.
  ENDIF.
  PERFORM protocol_update_return USING <ls_return>.
ENDLOOP.
IF lv_error = 'X'.
  returncode = 1.
  use_screen = ' '.
ELSE.
  returncode = 0.
ENDIF.

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  PERFORM protocol_update.
ENDIF.
ENDFORM.                    " ENTRY

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       FORM PROTOCOL_UPDATE                                          *
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       The messages are collected for the processing protocol.       *
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FORM protocol_update.

  CALL FUNCTION 'NAST_PROTOCOL_UPDATE'
    EXPORTING
      msg_arbgb = syst-msgid
      msg_nr    = syst-msgno
      msg_ty    = syst-msgty
      msg_v1    = syst-msgv1
      msg_v2    = syst-msgv2
      msg_v3    = syst-msgv3
      msg_v4    = syst-msgv4
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS    = 1.

ENDFORM.                    "PROTOCOL_UPDATE
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Form  protocol_update_return
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*      -->P_<LS_RETURN>  text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM protocol_update_return USING p_return TYPE bapiret2.

  CALL FUNCTION 'NAST_PROTOCOL_UPDATE'
    EXPORTING
      msg_arbgb = p_return-id
      msg_nr    = p_return-number
      msg_ty    = p_return-type
      msg_v1    = p_return-message_v1
      msg_v2    = p_return-message_v2
      msg_v3    = p_return-message_v3
      msg_v4    = p_return-message_v4
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS    = 1.

ENDFORM.                    " protocol_update_return

7. Final Architecture Principle

Standard-first approach

Low-coding strategy

Dynamic control via enhancement

Output-driven automation

Controlled customer-based exclusion

Lock-safe execution

8. Conclusion

This solution delivers a fully automated Order → Delivery → Goods Issue → Billing process while maintaining:

  • High adherence to SAP standards
  • Minimal custom development footprint
  • Flexible customer-level control
  • System stability and scalability

By combining standard configuration (VOV8, VV21), enhancement logic (MV45AFZZ), and output-based processing (REA0), an end-to-end automated and maintainable SD process was successfully implemented.

entry.title: Re: how can create an app variante using adapt UI and ass...
entry.content:
Good day @Hichem_Rahmani 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, you don’t solve the issue you described by creating a new business catalog for the Adapt UI app variant. The reason is, app variant of a standard app is typically not handled like a separate custom app catalog object that you can freely place into a new business catalog to control authorisation.

The following reference provides some insights into that situation: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4fc8d03390c342da8a60f8ee387bca1a/91ae3492323b42a79ca66fbfaf5af3f9.html

Instead of that, I suggest you use business roles plus space/page to control who see the app variant tile.

You can set this up by:

  • Keeping the standard Manage Business Partner app in the roles assigned to your users.
  • Creating a separate custom busines role for the users who should use the variant.
  • In that custom role, assigning a custom launchpad space.
  • Adding the app variant tile to that page and assigning that custom role only to your selected users.

See the following references on creating spaces for more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4fc8d03390c342da8a60f8ee387bca1a/f18398d8c95f400fa01ae9794e660733.html

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/performing-advanced-configuration-and-role-management-in-sap-s4hana-cloud-public-edition-ehs-environment-management/managing-spaces-and-pages

Note that, the setup mentioned above only controls who sees the variant in the launchpad. It does not mean a hard technical authorisation separation of the variant from the base app in every case. There are some capabilities in the new release that supports IAM App authorisation variants. See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/61f5a1ac89e04b68aaf755c883f1bbac.html

To summarise your specific question:

  • Use Custom Business Role,
  • Manage Launchpad Spaces and,
  • Assign only the variant tile to the selected role.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Activation Error in Repair Order
entry.content:
The task S4DK903349 of your transport request is corrupted. It is of type Dev/Correction but contains an object whose source system is not S4D (as you say, it's SAP), which means that the object is repaired and consequently it must be in a repair task.

"Move" the object from the Dev/Correction task to the repair task (delete + insert).

For information, the repair tasks can contain only repaired objects (source system <> current system).

Make sure that all objects in your transport request belong to the right type of task, then you can activate.

NB: I guess that when you activate, the system looks for a task where any subobject of the class is located and updates it. I saw this kind of logic in F2814 Manage KPI when changing one KPI (R3TR SSB1/SSB9 entry) although it was adding in a task of type Dev/Correction. In any case, the system verifies that the system source of the added/updated object matches the type of task.

entry.title: Re: Pricing in Service PO according to service specification
entry.content:
You can maintain outline agreements (contracts) with condition records and scales (e.g., thickness ranges, weight brackets). When you create a Service PO, the system can pull the net price from the contract item. Limitation: This only gives you a single net price per PO item. It does not automatically explode into multiple service lines in ESLL.
SAP does not have a standard mechanism where service specifications (attributes) drive automatic determination of which ESLL line applies. The system cannot “know” which detailed price line to pick unless you manually enter or build a custom enhancement.

You can consider using detailed service master records (with unique service codes for each attribute combination) so that the PO item itself directly reflects the correct price.

Integration with SD/Service Orders: In scenarios where service pricing is complex, companies often manage pricing in SD (Sales & Distribution) or Service Management, where condition technique is more powerful, and then integrate with MM. You can define condition tables with multiple key fields (e.g., thickness, grade, width). Access sequences can determine the correct condition record automatically. Pricing procedures can combine multiple condition types (base price, surcharges, discounts). This allows SD to calculate a highly detailed service price automatically, based on attributes.

Subsequently, in Service Management (SM) or Customer Service (CS), you create a service order in SD. The service order uses SD pricing to determine the correct price. When external procurement is needed, the system can generate a purchase requisition or purchase order in MM. The net value flows from SD pricing into MM documents.

From integration point of view,

  • Account Assignment & Settlement: Costs from MM PO are settled back to the SD service order.
  • Value Flow: The SD service order carries the pricing logic; MM PO reflects the agreed value but does not re‑calculate detailed pricing.
  • Contracts: You can still use MM contracts for vendor agreements, but the “brains” of pricing sit in SD.

For any reasons, if this is not feasible, consider by creating granular service master codes (e.g., “Cutting – Thickness 10–20mm”). Each service code has its own condition record in MM contracts. This way, MM alone can handle pricing, but it requires a large service catalog.

entry.title: How in SAP S/4 HANA public cloud legal requirement for threshold based tax rates
entry.content:
Hi,

Can anyone explain how in public cloud Tax rates is managed as per threshold based on the 2025/2026 as one product with different rates as per threshold, below are the examples

1. Apparel and Clothing
5% GST: Applied to clothing with a sale value of up to Rs. 2,500 per piece.
12% or Higher: Apparel priced above this threshold generally attracts higher rates (formerly 12%, but moving towards 18% in the new 2025 framework for high-value items). 

2. Footwear
5% GST: Applied to footwear with a Retail Sale Price (RSP) not exceeding Rs. 500 per pair.
18% GST: Applied to footwear with a sale value exceeding Rs. 500 per pair. 

3. Footwear & Ready-made Garments (General Value Rule)
As per CBIC guidelines, readymade garments with a transaction value up to Rs. 1,000 per piece attract 5% GST, while those above Rs. 1,000 attract 12%. 

4. Luxury and High-Value Items (40% Slab)
Under the new GST 2.0 regime effective from September 22, 2025, a high-value threshold (e.g., luxury cars, premium motorcycles over 350cc) triggers the最高 (highest) tax slab of 40%, replacing the previous 28% + cess structure

Kindly help

entry.title: Re: Inspection Lot Quantity field's value is not printing...
entry.content:
Step 1: Debug the BAdI during certificate generation and confirm whether QALS-MENGE (or the relevant field) is populated.
Step 2: If populated, ensure you are mapping it into the output structure (CS_CERTIFICATE).
Step 3: Re-check the Adobe form binding — sometimes the field name in the form does not match the structure element.
Step 4: If the lot quantity is not available in QALS, check inspection lot completion and batch linkage

entry.title: Monitor para consumo de mensajes en Integration Suite
entry.content:
1.- El consumo de mensajes en SAP BTP Integration Suite

SAP BTP Integration Suite es, sin duda, uno de los elementos más utilizados dentro de los servicios provistos por SAP BTP, especialmente cuando se trata de una implementación de S4HANA, en la que el bus de integración se hace un elemento crítico a la hora de soportar múltiples procesos de negocio.

No es difícil entonces, que con el paso del tiempo, un mismo tenant de Integration Suite tenga dentro de sus flujos de integración, múltiples elementos que pertenezcan a diferentes líneas de negocio y que tienen una característica en común, el consumo de mensajes.

Dentro de las herramientas provistas por el cockpit central de SAP BTP, tenemos información general con respecto al consumo de mensajes de correspondientes a diferentes aplicaciones que Integration Suite provee (integration flows, open connectors, event mesh, etc), pero solamente podemos ver a nivel de tenant:

💡 No solamente los integration flows creados en Integration Suite consumen mensajes al momento de ser ejecutados. La nota SAP 2942344](https://me.sap.com/notes/2942344), entrega un detalle acerca del consumo de mensajes, casos en que son contabilizados y su ponderación dentro de Integration Suite

El desafío comienza cuando necesitamos información detallada con respecto al consumo específico de mensajes por cada elemento utilizado, en este caso, por cada integration-flow o si necesitamos ir más lejos, conocer el consumo de mensajes por cada artefacto dentro de un integration flow. Cómo hacemos eso?

 

2.- SAP Cloud Integration API

Integration Suite, como muchos de los entitlements que podemos activar dentro de SAP BTP, tiene la capacidad de disponibilizar acceso seguro a algunas de sus funcionalidades, dentro de ellas a información detallada de todo elemento, proceso y artefacto que es ejecutado dentro de la suite.

La API que utilizaremos es SAP Cloud Integration esta provista en formato Odata V2 y nos entregará acceso al detalle interno de nuestro tenant de Integration Suite:

💡Si necesitas conocer mas acerca del contenido provisto por la api de Integration Suite, puedes encontrar todo el detalle dentro de SAP Business Accelerator Hub (https://api.sap.com)  específicamente en este Link 

 

3.- Configuración

Para poder acceder a las API de integration suite, necesitaremos crear nuestras credenciales especiales de seguridad y contar con una instancia de Process Integration Runtime, que es un entitlement que puede activarse solamente cuando se tiene una instancia de Integration Suite activa. Process Integration Runtime tiene dos planes de activación:

- api - para poder acceder a los endpoints con información operacional dentro de la suite.
- Integration-flow - para poder realizar operaciones como la ejecución de un flujo de integración desde un ente externo, como una aplicación o en respuesta a una alerta.

3.1.- Asignación del entitlement

En este caso necesitamos el plan de api, por. lo que asignaremos en el entitlement a la misma subcuenta donde está el tenant de integration suite que nos interesa monitorear.

Realizamos la asignación desde nuestra cuenta global a la subcuenta que nos interesa solo seleccionando el plan api, guardamos los cambios y nos dirigimos a la subcuenta con la que estamos trabajando.

3.2.- Creación de la instancia

Dentro de la subcuenta, en la sección **Service Marketplace**, encontraremos el entitlement disponible:

Solo necesitamos seleccionar el servicio y hacer click en el botón **Create** con las siguientes opciones:

⚠️Cuando se activa un servicio de Integration Suite en una subcuenta nueva, no es requisito mandatorio activar Cloud Foundry a nivel de subcuenta. Pero la creación de una instancia de Process Integration Runtime necesitará el environment activo y también un espacio exclusivo para su uso.

Después de dar click en Next aparecen las opciones relacionadas con el tipo de acceso que estaremos generando para poder consumir la API desde entidades externas a Integration suite. De acuerdo al modelo de seguridad de SAP BTP, utilizaremos el modelo Oauth2 generando client credentials (ClientId + ClientSecret) que generarán un token de autenticación para las los requerimientos enviados a la API. Adicionalmente es necesario asignar los roles que tendrá la instancia para consumir datos, en este caso solamente nos interesa consumir los endpoints provistos por la API Message Processing Logs. Esto se consigue con el Rol Monitoring Data Read como se puede ver en la imagen:

Finalmente el sistema presentará un resumen de la instancia que estaremos creando y se iniciará el proceso de creación.

3.3.- Creación de Service Key

Luego de algunos minutos, se termina la creación de la instancia y podemos acceder crear una llave ClientId / ClientSecret para poder realizar consultas externas.

En el menú de tres puntos al final de la línea, seleccionamos la opción Create Service Key, poblamos nuestros datos y solicitamos crear la llave:

💡En este caso no estamos utilizando certificados externos, solamente vamos a autenticar por token, pero si es requerido, al momento de crear las llaves puedes hacer uso de tu certificado. Esto no es exclusivo de Process Integration Runtime y la opción existe para toda llave de acceso a instancia que requieras generar en BTP.

Después de algunos segundos tendremos la llave creada y podemos acceder a la información de conexión:

Accedemos al menú de view:

Y tenemos las credenciales para acceder en forma externa a nuestra api.

💡Recuerda que existe una url para acceder a los endpoints y un tokenUrl para realizar el proceso de obtención de un token que tendremos que enviar en los headers de nuestra consulta.

❗ En algunas versiones anteriores de service key o para otros servicios, era necesario agregar al final del tokenUrl **/oauth/token** en este caso ya viene incluido en la url, no es necesario replicarlo.

4.- Accediendo a la API

Para acceder a la API de Integration Suite, necesitamos cualquier HTTP Client que nos permita enviar peticiones soportando primero la obtención del token y después agregándolo como Bearer <token> dentro de los headers de la petición. Pueden utilizar cualquier cliente HTTP. Si no tienen acceso a ninguno y están trabajando con Build o Business Application Studio, pueden utilizar el cliente HTTP integrado en el IDE de igual forma.

4.1.- Configuración del cliente HTTP

En el caso de los clientes HTTP modernos, la configuración la podemos realizar en el segménto AUTH de la colección o la llamada en específico.

Los pasos a seguir son:

- Seleccionar el tipo de autenticación OAuth2.0
- Agregar las credenciales de acuerdo a nuestra llave de acceso y es muy importante que la opción sobre donde se agregarán las credenciales sea Basic Auth Header porque no queremos que el token viaje en el cuerpo de la petición. Su configuración debiese lucir similar a esto:

  • Oauth 2.0 :
    • Grant Type: Client Credentials
  • Configuration :
    • Access Token Url --> Token Url de nuestra service key
    • Client ID --> Client Id de nuestra service key
    • Client Secret --> Client Secret de nuestra service key
    • Add Credentials to: Basic Auth Header

💡Siempre que utilicemos clientes que centralizan las credenciales de acceso a otros sitios, es recomendable que utilicemos variables de entorno para no estar utilizando los datos en duro dentro de cada petición.

Con respecto a la información del token, es necesario darle un ID, que sea agregado a los Headers de la petición y que tenga el prefijo estándar Bearer:

  • Token
    • Token ID --> <Nombre para identificar el token generado>
    • Add token to --> Headers
    • Header Prefix --> Bearer

Finalmente, damos click en el botón Get Access Token y tendremos el registro de nuestro token con las autorizaciones y scope que le dimos a nuestra instancia de Process Integration Runtime.

Este token se debe utilizar como un parámetro en los headers de las peticiones, siempre llevando el prefijo Bearer. Si nuestro cliente HTTP no tiene la capacidad de asignarlo directamente, es necesario configurarlo en forma manual. 

❗Recuerda que los token tienen validez de algunas horas. Generalmente los clientes HTTP pueden dar la opcion de renovar en caso de expiración, pero si no es el caso o si lo vas a trabajar a nivel de una aplicación, tienes que considerar que se debe verificar la validez del token antes de cada llamada a realizar.

4.2.- Creando la llamada HTTP

Finalmente solo nos queda crear la llamada HTTP Get hacia la url que nos dio nuestra llave mas el endpoint que nos interesa consumir. En este caso para traer los message processing logs, nuestra url será:

http:// {{baseUrl}}/api/v1/MessageProcessingLogs?$inlinecount=allpages 

💡En este caso `{{baseUrl}}`es el campo url definido en nuestra service key, está en ese formato porque lo estoy utilizando como una variable de entorno, si vas a poner el valor en duro, copia el contenido del campo url desde la service key y omite el uso de {{ }}.

💡 Puedes revisar los endpoints disponibles para Message Processing Logs en este Link 

Como dejamos la autenticación en forma centralizada, no tenemos que agregar nada más al http request y en este caso solo como opcional, dejamos el parámetro `inlinecount=allpages`para tener la información un poco mas completa.

Finalmente, tenemos un archivo JSON con la información de los mensajes, contenido, estatus y flujo de integración al que pertenece, con esta información ya lo que queda es poder tabular los datos para tener una presentación más clara:

5.- La capa de presentación

La API provista por Integration Suite es muy completa en información técnica con respecto a elementos, estados de procesos, tiempos de ejecución y teniendo ya el acceso, no es complejo desarrollar un backend que soporte los dos pasos del handshake de autenticación y pueda presentar un monitor muy certero y user friendly con la información de los mensajes procesados dentro de integration suite sin necesidad de acceder directamente al tenant dentro de SAP BTP. De la misma forma la API nos provee de suficientes campos de control para filtrar el contenido en caso de que necesitemos hacerlo.

En mi caso lo quise dejar con un look mas Fiori, por si después se requiere publicar en un WorkZone y mantener la integridad de formato.

Espero que este blog les haya sido de ayuda.

Mis saludos.

Rodrigo Riveros A.

entry.title: Re: IBP Phase Out Issues - moving unwanted volume
entry.content:
Why this happens is because,

Aggregation logic: IBP treats the product group as the forecasting level. When some members phase out, the group forecast is preserved, and the system reallocates volume to the remaining members.

Phase‑Out flag: End dates tell IBP when a product should stop receiving forecast, but they don’t tell IBP to reduce the group forecast itself.

Default redistribution: By design, IBP redistributes demand proportionally (or fully, if only one product remains).

Options to Control Phase‑Out

1. Use Phase‑Out Profiles
IBP supports Phase‑Out profiles similar to Phase‑In. Instead of abruptly ending, you can define a gradual ramp‑down curve (e.g., linear decline, percentage drop). This prevents sudden redistribution spikes.

2. Exclude Products from Reference
In the forecast model configuration, you can exclude certain products from being used as reference for future demand. Mark the product as “Do not use for forecast reference” once it’s phasing out. This way, IBP won’t push group demand into it.

3. Adjust Forecast Level
Forecast at a lower level (material ID) instead of product group, then aggregate up. This avoids redistribution logic at group level.

Downside: less smoothing across items.

4. Use Attribute-Based Phase‑Out
Maintain a product attribute (e.g., “Phase‑Out = Y”). Configure forecast disaggregation rules to ignore items with this flag. This requires a small modeling change but gives precise control.

5. Manual Override / Planning Operator
As a last resort, planners can override the forecast for the phasing‑out item to zero. Or use a custom planning operator to redistribute demand to a different product family instead of the remaining item.

entry.title: SAP Job Scheduling Service: We Redesigned the Dashboard (And Added Analytics)
entry.content:
We Redesigned the Dashboard (And Added Analytics)

Fewer clicks. More insights. Still in preview — we need your feedback.

Hi folks 👋

You may have already seen it, but we put some love into our Job Scheduling Dashboard.

It's now more intuitive - we've eliminated some pages so you can navigate with fewer clicks - saving you precious time. And we've standardized the look and feel, so you can easily find what you need.

But the bigger change? We're experimenting with analytics. 📊

Many of you have told us you use the dashboard for monitoring your jobs - but honestly, it doesn't always do the job well. One example we kept hearing: "How do I find if some execution failed in a schedule?" Good question. We didn't have a good answer. So we built one. 🛠️

We want to give you more insights into your jobs, so you can easily identify issues and optimize your workflows. But this is just the beginning - we have many more ideas in the pipeline, and we can't wait to share them with you.

These features are in preview, so we'd love your feedback. Some of them are limited to certain regions or depend on the number of jobs in your instance. Let us know what works and what doesn't.

The Overview Page

What's new here:

  • Configuration Page is gone - it's now a simple edit right in this page (one less click, you're welcome)
  • Two statistics cards - one for Job Statistics and one for Schedule Statistics

 

Jobs

In the Jobs overview we decided to show the filter by default. You have a filter??? Yes we have - but it was kind of hidden from you. We know. Sorry about that. 😅 Apart from the filter, only the layout has changed.

In the Job details page we merged the overview with schedules - everything in one place now.

But here's the interesting part - we've added analytics cards. Schedule statistics. Best practices warnings. Like when you have a POST job without a body defined in the schedule - because trust me, that leads to issues on your side. We've seen it happen too many times. ⚠️

Schedule Overview

Same idea as Jobs - we merged the overview and details pages into one.

Here we thought you might be interested in the success rate of your schedules, so we added a card for that. Simple question, simple answer: are your schedules healthy or not?

Help Us Make It Better 💬

This is preview for a reason - your feedback shapes what comes next.

Now it's your turn. Try the new features and tell us what you think. What's working? What's broken? What's missing? We're listening. 👂

  • Comment here - post a comment or send a message
  • Influence Portal - BTP Foundation - help shape the roadmap
  • Documentation feedback - Help Portal or GitHub
  • Reach out directly - I'm Denis, the PM for Job Scheduling service. Find me on LinkedIn - always happy to chat.

entry.title: Re: Pricing in Service PO according to service specification
entry.content:
SAP standard pricing can determine the net price at item level, but attribute-based automatic service line creation in ESLL requires either catalog integration or custom development.

  • Contracts/Info Records
      : You can maintain service conditions at a high level. The PO will adopt the net price if the contract item matches.
    • Service Master (AC03): Allows you to define service codes and link them to valuation classes, but it does not handle attribute-based price determination.
    • Condition Technique (MM/SD Pricing): Standard SAP pricing can determine a single net price based on condition records (e.g., thickness range as a condition key). However, this works at the PO item level, not at the ESLL line level.

ESLL Lines Require Manual Entry

  • The Service Specification (ESLL) is designed to capture detailed service components (sub-services, activities, resources).
  • SAP does not “know” which combination of attributes applies unless you explicitly define them in the PO or contract.
  • Hence, manual entry is needed unless you automate via enhancements.

Automation

  1. Service Catalog Integration (SRM/Ariba or external catalog)
    • You can integrate a service catalog where attributes drive the price. When the user selects “Cutting – thickness 10–20mm,” the system transfers the correct ESLL line with price.
  2. Custom Condition Tables + Access Sequence
    • Define a condition table with service attributes (thickness, grade, etc.) as key fields.
    • Maintain condition records for each combination.
    • Use a custom routine to explode these into ESLL lines automatically when the PO is created.
  3. BAdI/Enhancement
    • Implement logic to read service attributes from the PO (or a custom field).
    • Derive the correct ESLL lines and populate them programmatically.
    • This is the most common approach in projects where detailed service pricing is attribute-driven.

  • Easy Cost Planning / Project System Integration
    • If services are linked to projects, you can use Easy Cost Planning to define detailed cost structures and transfer them into the PO. This is more common in engineering/procurement scenarios.

Recommendation

  • Standard SAP cannot fully automate ESLL population based on attributes.
  • The closest standard approach is using contracts with detailed service lines (each attribute combination as a separate line).
  • For dynamic attribute-driven pricing, you need either:
  • Catalog integration, or
  • Custom enhancement (BAdI) to generate ESLL lines automatically.

Hello Nagadatt,

This can be done using transaction code OBA5. Go to application area F5, locate message number 117, and change the Online column to “E"

Also, check the customization under the following path:

SPRO → Materials Management → Logistics Invoice Verification → Incoming Invoice → Set Check for Duplicate Invoices

There, select and activate the checkbox for the relevant company codes.

Once this is activated, the system will prevent duplicate invoices by checking the reference, invoice date, and company code.

entry.title: Re: I need a process for bulk liquid deliveries with mult...
entry.content:
Transportation Management (TM) Integration
In S/4HANA with SAP TM, you can model a single freight order with multiple delivery stops. TM handles the route and stop sequence; ERP deliveries remain customer‑specific. Goods issue is posted per delivery, aligned with the stop.

Advantage: True “multi‑stop” truck scenario supported.
Limitation: Requires TM license and setup.

Batch Split / Handling Units
Use batch split or handling units in the delivery to represent each drop quantity. Post goods issue per HU. Billing can be aligned to HU quantities.

Advantage: Flexible, especially if product is tracked by batch.
Limitation: More complex warehouse execution.

Multiple Deliveries for One Shipment
Create separate outbound deliveries for each customer/stop‑off. Use shipment/transportation planning to combine them into one truckload. Each delivery posts goods issue independently, so billing is clean per customer.

Advantage: Standard process, no manual sales order changes.
Limitation: More delivery documents to manage.

Delivery Split by Item
Create one delivery with multiple line items (each representing a stop‑off quantity). Post goods issue per item. Billing picks up each item separately.

Advantage: Fewer documents than creation multiple deliveries for one shipment.
Limitation: Requires careful item setup to match stop‑off sequence.

entry.title: Re: Third Party Sales Order Processing in SAP S4HANA Clou...
entry.content:
Good day @SAPUser007_

Scenario 1:

Attempt 1: even if you manually enter components later in the PO, this is still originating from a third party, not a true MM subcontracting procurement flow.

Attempt 2: this works because you are creating the procurement flow directly as a real subcontracting process and the cockpit can show the component requirements.

Ticking the recursive BOM indicator does not convert the SD created PR flow into a standard subcontracting scenario that is relevant for the cockpit. The cockpit’s visibility depends on the subcontracting procurement structure and component requirement logic and not just on having a BOM.

As previously mentioned, I still recommend you:

  1. Create subcontracting PO directly with item category L, maintain component list/BOM explosion and use Subcontracting Cockpit for component supply and monitoring
  2. If the same material is sent out and comes back after rework, model it as external service or rework process. Don’t force subcontracting just to get automatic PR from SO.

Scenario 2:

Standard third-party sales item category setup is generally, in Public Cloud, aimed at material-based procurement from SD. For a service item, you may not get a standard third-party relevant item category in your configuration.

So, you are not necessarily doing it wrong.

Use Item 10 as AC material, Item 20 for installation service and procurement of the installation service is handled via purchasing based on your implemented service procurement scope, not necessarily classic SD third party item category logic.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Batch determination based on WBS BOM item characteris...
entry.content:
Why it happens is because, batch determination in PP/CO is driven by strategy records (plant/movement type, etc.). The system looks at batch characteristics in the batch master, but it does not automatically inherit or compare against classification values maintained in the BOM line. Reservation items (from WBS BOM) don’t pass characteristic filters into the determination logic.

Options to Restrict to One Batch

1. Batch Selection Criteria (CUOBJ) Enhancement
Extend the reservation item so that the classification values from the BOM line are written into the reservation (CUOBJ). Then enhance the batch determination BAdI (BAdI MB_MIGO_BADI or BAdI VBSK_BATCH_DET) to read those values and filter batches accordingly. This way, the system only proposes the batch that matches the BOM line’s characteristic.

2. Batch Search Strategy with User Exit
Standard batch search strategies don’t read BOM line classification. But you can use user exit EXIT_SAPMM07M_001 (or BAdI BATCH_SEARCH) to add custom selection logic. In the exit, read the reservation item’s classification values and restrict the batch list.

3. Manual Batch Assignment via CO27
As you’re doing now, you can manually pick the batch in CO27. This is the fallback if you don’t enhance the determination logic.

4. Alternative Master Data Approach
Instead of one material number for whole units and rest pieces, consider creating a separate material type or material number for rest pieces. Then batch determination can be simpler, because you’re not mixing two categories under one material.

entry.title: Re: Breaking SAP Data Barriers with Datasphere & Databricks: A Medallion Journey (Bronze, Silver
entry.content:
Great post on the conceptual integration, but I have a question regarding the 'Native Databricks' Medallion implementation.

As per the SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Connect terms and the Zero-Copy/Delta Sharing architecture, the data remains federated. My understanding is that this 'Zero Delta Share' approach specifically does not allow for persistent  materialization on the connected system (Databricks).

If we cannot physically land and merge data into Delta tables within Databricks, we effectively lose the ability to build a native Bronze/Silver/Gold pipeline where Databricks manages the ACID transactions and performance optimization.

In this scenario, would Premium Outbound Integration be a mandatory prerequisite to achieve the Medallion architecture described? Or is there a specific way to utilize BDC Connect that allows Databricks to 'own' the materialization without violating the BDC supplements?
