entry.title: Re: Create Planning objects for Price KF
entry.content: You’re not missing a concept — it’s just that planning objects are key‑figure specific. Lifecycle planning creates demand combinations, but revenue requires that the same product/location/customer combination also exists in the price key figure. Without that, IBP won’t generate revenue automatically.

entry.title: Re: how to change housebank all at once
entry.content:
Hello Misa,

In the payment proposal the house bank will be either the one provided in the document/BP or determined by ranking order. In the payment proposal you can select one or more items and change the housebank (HB), but all of them will be paid by this newly selected HB. It is not possible to select multiple proposal and provide them different HBs. Each of the proposal items may have different BBs when we run execute the payment run, so when we select multiple items, you will not be able to see their HBs in the pop-up screen, but you can select any HB here to change for all the selected items.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Breaking SAP Data Barriers with Datasphere & Databricks: A Medallion Journey (Bronze, Silver
entry.content:
Thank you@Sven_Knpfler for going over the blog!
your understanding is right on spot

BDC Connect + Delta Sharing = Federated, zero‑copy access.
By design, the shared dataset stays in SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), and Databricks receives live, governed, read‑optimized access to those data products — not a copy and not a materialized Delta Lake table. Below is the Overview of it how it connects.
https://help.sap.com/docs/business-data-cloud/sap-databricks/working-with-sap-databricks 

I will be preparing a new blog sooner on how this architecture looks like and how the end to end setup would look.

entry.title: Recurring Entry Approval Workflow in SAP S/4HANA - FBD1/FBD2/FBD9 Transactions
entry.content:
Introduction:

In many organizations, finance teams handle repetitive postings such as rent, subscriptions, insurance, lease payments, and service charges. Manually posting these entries every period increases operational effort and the risk of errors.

SAP provides the Recurring Entry Program (Transaction FBD1) to automate such postings. However, in controlled environments, organizations often require approval workflows before recurring documents are posted.

This blog explains how to implement and control a Recurring Entry Workflow using FBD1, ensuring automation with proper financial governance.

Requirement:

In SAP Standard, through FBD1 users can create recurring document templates. But these templates cannot be posted automatically. The created recurring document templates must be posted via F.14 transaction. It happens periodically through Job Scheduling.

FBD1 does not include any approval mechanism by standard. This leads to

  • Unauthorized financial commitments.
  • Incorrect Recurring Postings.
  • Audit, Compliance & Seggregation of Duties (SoD) violations.

To mitigate these risks, business requires an approval mechanism, so that

  • Authorized user only creates Recurring Document Templates (FBD1).
  • Delegation of Authority (DoA) ensures proper screening before approval.
  • Approved entries alone will be considered for Posting Runs.

Solution Architecture:

The proposed solution will work on triggering an approval workflow when the user creates a Recurring Document Entry through FBD1 and when he modifies the rejected Recurring Document Entry through FBD2. Modifications of the Recurring Document Template when it is in Approval is prevented through Custom Control Error Mechanism.

Process Flow:

Development Process:

Step – 1: Create a Recurring Document Entry Template in FBD1 T-Code. The Entry must be blocked, so that only upon complete approval of workflow block shall be removed and would process for posting.

For this purpose, we must enhance the standard procedure through Business Transaction Events. To identify the suitable BTE’s that can be used for our purpose, we can use SE37 transaction with the FM “PC_FUNCTION_FIND”. Keep a Session Breakpoint in the FM and run the process. The Parameter “I_EVENT” will provide the BTE numbers from which we can choose the suitable one for our purpose.

NOTE: For Blocking the Document while creation, through the above-mentioned process we came to the BTE as “SAMPLE_INTERFACE_00001025”.

Step – 2: Take a copy of the FM “SAMPLE_INTERFACE_00001025” in SE37. I have kept the name as “ZFI_BKDF_DOC_BLOCK_FM”.

Copy and paste the below code inside the FM.

DATA:
     lv_memory_bkdf TYPE char30 VALUE '(SAPMF05A)BKDF',
     lv_memory_bkdf1 TYPE char30 VALUE '(SAPMF05L)BKDF',
     wa_bkpf TYPE bkpf.
FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lfs_bkdf> TYPE bkdf.
READ TABLE t_bkpf into wa_bkpf index 1.
    If sy-tcode = 'FBD1' or sy-tcode = 'FBD9'.
      ASSIGN (lv_memory_bkdf) TO <lfs_bkdf>.
    elseif sy-tcode = 'FBD2'.
      ASSIGN (lv_memory_bkdf1) TO <lfs_bkdf>.
    endif.
   IF <lfs_bkdf> IS ASSIGNED AND t_bkpf-bukrs EQ '9000'.
    <lfs_bkdf>-blocked_for_posting = abap_true.
    <lfs_bkdf>-XDELT = abap_true.
  ENDIF.

NOTE:

  • Here “BLOCKED_FOR_POSTING” & “XDELT (Deletion_Indicator) are the fields for which value has been to “X” for blocking the document.
  • The runtime values of these fields are fetched from previous procedures through Memory Assignment.

For FBD1 & FBD2, the standard procedural program varies (See Data Declaration).

Step – 3: Once the Recurring Entry Document is blocked and saved, our custom workflow must get triggered. Before proceeding to Workflow, we must create a custom class where the Event, Methods necessary for the workflow operation would reside.

  1. Go to the T-code SE24 and create a custom class. I had created in the name “ZCL_FI_RECURRING_WF”.  In the Interfaces tab, provide the interfaces as shown in the below image. These interfaces are necessary for Object instantiation & Workflow Operations.
  2. In the Events Tab, I have given the event name as “CREATED” as shown below.
    The event parameter is “ZST_WF_REC_JOUR”. It is a custom table type referring a custom structure "     ZFI_ST_RECJOUR_ENTRY”.

    Structure:
  3. In the Attributes tab, Object Key is specified as “LV_OBJKEY” as shown below.
  4. The standard methods corresponding to the added interfaces, would reflect in the Methods tab as shown below.
  5. Now we can add our custom methods. The following are the methods that I created for my requirement as shown below.
    M_FETCH_APPROVER – Fetching Approvers and Sending Approval Notifications.
    M_UNBLOCK_POSTING – Unblocking the Recurring Entry Document after Complete Approval.
    M_COMPLETE_MAIL – Notification Mail to Initiator after Complete Approval / Rejection in any level.
  6. The individual method procedures and its parameters are below.
    M_FETCH_APPROVER:
    Parameters:

    Here, “ZTT_FI_WF_AGENT” table type is a single field for storing the agents (Image represents structure of the Table Type).
     “ZFI_RECUR_MAT_APP_TT” table type is for storing agent information (Image represents structure of the Table Type).
DATA:
  gc_subject     TYPE so_obj_des, " Email subject
  gc_email_to    TYPE adr6-smtp_addr, " Valid email
  gs_text        TYPE soli, " Text used into the email body
  gc_type_raw    TYPE so_obj_tp, " Email type
  gc_att_type    TYPE soodk-objtp, " Attachment type
  gc_att_subject TYPE sood-objdes. " Attachment title

  DATA:
  gt_text           TYPE soli_tab, " Table which contains email body text
  gt_attachment_hex TYPE solix_tab, " Table which contains the attached file
  gv_sent_to_all    TYPE os_boolean, " Receive the information if email was sent
  gv_error_message  TYPE string, " Used to get the error message
  go_send_request   TYPE REF TO cl_bcs, " Email object
  go_recipient      TYPE REF TO if_recipient_bcs, " Who will receive the email
  go_sender         TYPE REF TO cl_sapuser_bcs, " Who is sending the email
  go_document       TYPE REF TO cl_document_bcs, " Email body
  gx_bcs_exception  TYPE REF TO cx_bcs,
  wa_agents type ZST_FI_APPWF_AGENT.
  wa_rec_wf_sts type ZFI_REC_WF_ST_DT.

    lv_initiator = lv_initiator_us+2(12).
    lv_counter = lv_counter + 1.

     CALL METHOD zcl_amdp_fi_recurring_wf=>get_initial_approvers
      EXPORTING
        p_mandt      = sy-mandt
        p_compcode   = lv_comp_code
        p_init       = lv_initiator
      IMPORTING
        it_approvers = data(it_approvers).

    sort it_approvers by zlevel .
    delete ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM it_approvers comparing zlevel.
    DESCRIBE TABLE it_approvers LINES lv_counter_max.
    read table it_approvers into data(wa_approver) index 1.
    if sy-subrc = 0.
      lv_init_name = wa_approver-name_text1.
      lv_init_mid = wa_approver-smtp_addr1.
    endif.
    read table it_approvers into wa_approver with key zlevel = lv_counter.
    if sy-subrc = 0.
      concatenate 'US' wa_approver-zuser into wa_agents-agent.
      lv_appr_mid = wa_approver-smtp_addr.
      lv_appr_name = wa_approver-name_text.
      append wa_agents to it_agents.
    endif.

   TRY.
    go_send_request = cl_bcs=>create_persistent( ).
    go_sender = cl_sapuser_bcs=>create( 'SAP_WFRT' ).
    go_send_request->set_sender( i_sender = go_sender ).

  DATA(lv_belnr1) = lv_belnr.
  SHIFT lv_belnr1 LEFT DELETING LEADING '0'.

  CONCATENATE 'Approve Jounral Entry' lv_comp_code '-' lv_belnr1 '-' lv_gjahr INTO gc_subject SEPARATED BY space.

  gs_text-line = '<HTML><BODY>'.
  APPEND gs_text to gt_text[].
  CLEAR: gs_text.

  concatenate 'Dear' lv_appr_name into gs_text-line SEPARATED BY SPACE.
  APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
  CLEAR : gs_text.

  gs_text-line = '<br></br>'.
  APPEND gs_text TO gt_text.
  CLEAR: gs_text.

  concatenate 'Recurring Journal Entry' lv_belnr1 '-'  lv_comp_code '-' lv_gjahr 'is waiting for your approval.' into gs_text-line separated by space .
  APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
  CLEAR : gs_text.

  gs_text-line = '<br></br>'.
  APPEND gs_text TO gt_text.
  CLEAR: gs_text.

  gs_text-line = 'Please review the details using the provided link.'.
  APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
  CLEAR : gs_text.

  gs_text-line = '<br></br>'.
  APPEND gs_text TO gt_text.
  CLEAR: gs_text.

  gs_text-line = 'This is a system generated mail. Please do not reply to this.'.
  APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
  CLEAR: gs_text.

  gs_text-line = '<br></br>'.
  APPEND gs_text TO gt_text.
  CLEAR: gs_text.

  gs_text-line = 'Thanks and Regards,'.
  APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
  CLEAR: gs_text.

  gs_text-line = '<br></br>'.
  APPEND gs_text TO gt_text.
  CLEAR: gs_text.

  gs_text-line = lv_init_name.
  APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
  CLEAR: gs_text.

  gs_text-line = '</HTML></BODY>'.
  APPEND gs_text to gt_text[].
  CLEAR: gs_text.

  go_document = cl_document_bcs=>create_document(
                    i_type    = 'HTM'
                    i_text    = gt_text
                    i_subject = gc_subject ).

  go_send_request->set_document( go_document ).

*  loop at it_approvers into wa_approver.
    go_recipient = cl_cam_address_bcs=>create_internet_address( lv_appr_mid ).
    go_send_request->add_recipient(
          EXPORTING
            i_recipient = go_recipient
            i_express   = abap_true
        ).
*  endloop.

  gv_sent_to_all = go_send_request->send( i_with_error_screen = abap_true ).
  COMMIT WORK.
  CATCH cx_bcs INTO gx_bcs_exception.
    gv_error_message = gx_bcs_exception->get_text( ).
    WRITE gv_error_message.

ENDTRY.

IF LV_COUNTER > LV_COUNTER_MAX.
  LV_EXIT = 'X'.
ELSE.
  LV_EXIT = 'Y'.
ENDIF.

wa_rec_wf_sts-doc_no = lv_belnr1.
wa_rec_wf_sts-comp_code = lv_comp_code.
wa_rec_wf_sts-fis_year = lv_gjahr.
wa_rec_wf_sts-status = 'In-process'.

MODIFY ZFI_REC_WF_ST_DT from wa_rec_wf_sts.
COMMIT WORK.

M_UNBLOCK_POSTING:

Parameters:

    DATA:
      lt_bkdf       TYPE TABLE OF bkdf,
      lt_bkpf       TYPE TABLE OF bkpf,
      lt_dummy_bseg TYPE TABLE OF bseg,
      lt_dummy_bsec TYPE TABLE OF bsec,
      lt_dummy_bsed TYPE TABLE OF bsed,
      lt_dummy_bset TYPE TABLE OF bset.

    SELECT SINGLE *
    FROM bkpf
    INTO (ls_bkpf)
    WHERE bukrs = _bkdf-bukrs
    AND   belnr = _bkdf-belnr
    AND   gjahr = _bkdf-gjahr.

    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      " Read blocked recurring entry data
      SELECT SINGLE *
        FROM bkdf
        INTO (ls_bkdf)
        WHERE bukrs = _bkdf-bukrs
        AND   belnr = _bkdf-belnr
        AND   gjahr = _bkdf-gjahr.

      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        " Remove Block
        ls_bkdf-blocked_for_posting = abap_false.
        ls_bkdf-xdelt = abap_false.
        " Add records
        APPEND ls_bkpf TO lt_bkpf.
        APPEND ls_bkdf TO lt_bkdf.
        " Update document
        CALL FUNCTION 'CHANGE_DOCUMENT'
          TABLES
            t_bkdf = lt_bkdf
            t_bkpf = lt_bkpf
            t_bsec = lt_dummy_bsec
            t_bsed = lt_dummy_bsed
            t_bseg = lt_dummy_bseg
            t_bset = lt_dummy_bset.
        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          COMMIT WORK.
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

M_COMPLETE_MAIL:

Parameters:

        DATA: gc_subject     TYPE so_obj_des, " Email subject
          gc_email_to    TYPE adr6-smtp_addr, " Valid email
          gs_text        TYPE soli, " Text used into the email body
          gc_type_raw    TYPE so_obj_tp, " Email type
          gc_att_type    TYPE soodk-objtp, " Attachment type
          gc_att_subject TYPE sood-objdes. " Attachment title
    DATA:
          gt_text           TYPE soli_tab, " Table which contains email body text
          gt_attachment_hex TYPE solix_tab, " Table which contains the attached file
          gv_sent_to_all    TYPE os_boolean, " Receive the information if email was sent
          gv_error_message  TYPE string, " Used to get the error message
          go_send_request   TYPE REF TO cl_bcs, " Email object
          go_recipient      TYPE REF TO if_recipient_bcs, " Who will receive the email
          go_sender         TYPE REF TO cl_sapuser_bcs, " Who is sending the email
          go_document       TYPE REF TO cl_document_bcs, " Email body
          gx_bcs_exception  TYPE REF TO cx_bcs.

    DATA: e_reason                 TYPE swcont-value,
          reason_txt               TYPE swcont-value,
          l_lines                  TYPE i,
          object_content           TYPE TABLE OF solisti1,
          subcontainer_all_objects TYPE TABLE OF swr_cont,
          lv_wa_reason             LIKE LINE OF subcontainer_all_objects,
          lv_no_att                TYPE  sy-index,
          document_id              TYPE sofolenti1-doc_id,
          return_code              TYPE  sy-subrc,
          ifs_xml_container        TYPE  xstring,
          ifs_xml_container_schema TYPE  xstring,
          simple_container         TYPE TABLE OF swr_cont,
          message_lines            TYPE TABLE OF swr_messag,
          message_struct           TYPE TABLE OF swr_mstruc,
          subcontainer_bor_objects TYPE TABLE OF swr_cont.

    if lv_flag ne 'R'.
       TRY.

          go_send_request = cl_bcs=>create_persistent( ).
          go_sender = cl_sapuser_bcs=>create( 'SAP_WFRT' ).
          go_send_request->set_sender( i_sender = go_sender ).

CONCATENATE 'Recurring Entry Approved:' lv_comp_code lv_belnr lv_gjahr INTO gc_subject SEPARATED BY space.

          gs_text-line = '<HTML><BODY>'.
          APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
          CLEAR: gs_text.

          CONCATENATE 'Dear' lv_init_name ',' INTO gs_text-line SEPARATED BY space.
          APPEND gs_text TO gt_text.
          CLEAR: gs_text.

          gs_text-line = '<br></br>'.
          APPEND gs_text TO gt_text.
          CLEAR: gs_text.

          CONCATENATE 'Recurring Journal Entry' lv_belnr lv_comp_code lv_gjahr 'has been completely approved.' INTO gs_text-line SEPARATED BY space.
          APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
          CLEAR: gs_text.

          gs_text-line = '<br></br>'.
          APPEND gs_text TO gt_text.
          CLEAR: gs_text.

          CONCATENATE 'This is a system generated mail.' 'Please do not reply to this.' INTO gs_text-line SEPARATED BY space.
          APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
          CLEAR: gs_text.

          gs_text-line = '<br></br>'.
          APPEND gs_text TO gt_text.
          CLEAR: gs_text.

          gs_text-line = '</BODY></HTML>'.
          APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
          CLEAR: gs_text.

          go_document = cl_document_bcs=>create_document(
                            i_type    = 'HTM'
                            i_text    = gt_text
                            i_subject = gc_subject ).

          go_send_request->set_document( go_document ).


          go_recipient = cl_cam_address_bcs=>create_internet_address( lv_init_mid ).
          "Add recipient to send request
          go_send_request->add_recipient(
            EXPORTING
              i_recipient = go_recipient
              i_express   = abap_true
          ).

          "Send email and get the result
          gv_sent_to_all = go_send_request->send( i_with_error_screen = abap_true ).
          COMMIT WORK.
        CATCH cx_bcs INTO gx_bcs_exception.
          gv_error_message = gx_bcs_exception->get_text( ).
          WRITE gv_error_message.
      ENDTRY.

    else.
      DATA: lv_user TYPE bapibname-bapibname.
      DATA: ls_address TYPE bapiaddr3.
      DATA: lt_return TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2.

      lv_user = lv_actual_agent+2(12).

            CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_USER_GET_DETAIL'
              EXPORTING
                username = lv_user
              IMPORTING
                address  = ls_address
              TABLES
                return   = lt_return[].

           CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_WAPI_READ_CONTAINER'
            EXPORTING
              workitem_id              = lv_workitem_id
              language                 = sy-langu
              user                     = sy-uname
            IMPORTING
              return_code              = return_code
              ifs_xml_container        = ifs_xml_container
              ifs_xml_container_schema = ifs_xml_container_schema
            TABLES
              simple_container         = simple_container
              message_lines            = message_lines
              message_struct           = message_struct
              subcontainer_bor_objects = subcontainer_bor_objects
              subcontainer_all_objects = subcontainer_all_objects.
*      &-----------------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       Initialize
*      &-----------------------------------------------------------------------------*
          lv_no_att = 0.
*      &-----------------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       Read the _ATTACH_OBJECTS element
*      &-----------------------------------------------------------------------------*
          LOOP AT subcontainer_all_objects INTO lv_wa_reason
                                           WHERE element = 'DECISION_NOTE'."'_ATTACH_OBJECTS'.
            lv_no_att = lv_no_att + 1.
            document_id = lv_wa_reason-value.
          ENDLOOP.
*      &-----------------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       Read the SOFM Document
*      &-----------------------------------------------------------------------------*
          IF document_id IS NOT INITIAL.
            REFRESH : object_content.
            CALL FUNCTION 'SO_DOCUMENT_READ_API1'
              EXPORTING
                document_id                = document_id
*               FILTER                     = 'X '
*       IMPORTING
*               DOCUMENT_DATA              =
              TABLES
*               OBJECT_HEADER              =
                object_content             = object_content
*               OBJECT_PARA                =
*               OBJECT_PARB                =
*               ATTACHMENT_LIST            =
*               RECEIVER_LIST              =
*               CONTENTS_HEX               =
              EXCEPTIONS
                document_id_not_exist      = 1
                operation_no_authorization = 2
                x_error                    = 3
                OTHERS                     = 4.
            IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
              CLEAR : l_lines.
              DESCRIBE TABLE object_content LINES l_lines.
              IF l_lines LT 5.
                READ TABLE object_content INTO reason_txt INDEX 1.
                IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
                  e_reason =  reason_txt.
                ENDIF.
              ENDIF.
            ENDIF.
          ENDIF.

            TRY.
                "Create send request
                go_send_request = cl_bcs=>create_persistent( ).
                "Email FROM...
                go_sender = cl_sapuser_bcs=>create( 'SAP_WFRT' ).
                "Add sender to send request
                go_send_request->set_sender( i_sender = go_sender ).

                CONCATENATE 'Recurring Entry Rejected:' lv_comp_code lv_belnr lv_gjahr INTO gc_subject SEPARATED BY space.

                gs_text-line = '<HTML><BODY>'.
                APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
                CLEAR: gs_text.

                CONCATENATE 'Dear' lv_init_name ',' INTO gs_text-ine SEPARATED BY space.
                APPEND gs_text TO gt_text.
                CLEAR: gs_text.

                gs_text-line = '<br></br>'.
                APPEND gs_text TO gt_text.
                CLEAR: gs_text.

                CONCATENATE 'Journal Entry' lv_belnr lv_comp_code lv_gjahr 'has been rejeted by.' ls_address-fullname INTO gs_text-line SEPARATED BY space.
                APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
                CLEAR: gs_text.

                gs_text-line = '<br></br>'.
                APPEND gs_text TO gt_text.
                CLEAR: gs_text.

                CONCATENATE 'Rejection Reason:' e_reason INTO gs_text-line SEPARATED BY space.
                APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
                CLEAR: gs_text.

                gs_text-line = '<br></br>'.
                APPEND gs_text TO gt_text.
                CLEAR: gs_text.

                CONCATENATE 'This is a system generated mail.' 'Please do not reply to this.' INTO gs_text-line SEPARATED BY space.
                APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
                CLEAR: gs_text.

                gs_text-line = '<br></br>'.
                APPEND gs_text TO gt_text.
                CLEAR: gs_text.

                gs_text-line = '</BODY></HTML>'.
                APPEND gs_text TO gt_text[].
                CLEAR: gs_text.

                go_document = cl_document_bcs=>create_document(
                                  i_type    = 'HTM'
                                  i_text    = gt_text
*                          i_length  = '12'
                                  i_subject = gc_subject ).

                go_send_request->set_document( go_document ).
                go_recipient = cl_cam_address_bcs=>create_internet_address( lv_init_mid ).
                "Add recipient to send request
                go_send_request->add_recipient(
                  EXPORTING
                    i_recipient = go_recipient
                    i_express   = abap_true
                ).
                "Send email and get the result
                gv_sent_to_all = go_send_request->send( i_with_error_screen = abap_true ).
                COMMIT WORK.
              CATCH cx_bcs INTO gx_bcs_exception.
                gv_error_message = gx_bcs_exception->get_text( ).
                WRITE gv_error_message.
            ENDTRY.
    endif.
  endmethod.​

NOTE: Notification sent upon Complete Approval or upon Rejection in any one level is incorporated in the same method.

Thus, the class and methods required for the workflow operation has been completed successfully.

Step – 4: Now we need to trigger the Workflow once the Recurring Document is created in FBD1. For which we need to find the suitable BTE (For finding the BTE, refer the process mentioned in Step-2). The BTE for this requirement is “SAMPLE_INTERFACE_00001030”.
Copy this FM in SE37 to a custom one and write the below code.

IF sy-tcode = 'FBD1' OR sy-tcode = 'FBD9' .                 "Recurring Journal Entry Workflow Event Trigger

DATA: lv_objtype          TYPE sibftypeid,
      lv_event            TYPE sibfevent,
      lv_objkey           TYPE sibfinstid,
      lr_event_parameters TYPE REF TO if_swf_ifs_parameter_container,
      lv_param_name       TYPE swfdname,
      ls_recjour_input    TYPE zfi_st_recjour_entry.

CONSTANTS: lc_objtype    TYPE sibftypeid VALUE 'ZCL_FI_9000_RECURRING_WF',
           lc_event      TYPE sibfevent VALUE 'CREATED',
           lc_param_name TYPE swfdname VALUE 'ZST_WF_REC_JOUR'.

      lv_objtype = lc_objtype.
      lv_event   = lc_event.
      lv_param_name = lc_param_name.

      ls_recjour_input-wa_bkdf = i_bkdf.
      ls_recjour_input-wa_bkpf = t_bkpf.
      ls_recjour_input-wa_bsec = t_bsec.
      ls_recjour_input-wa_bsed = t_bsed.
      ls_recjour_input-wa_bseg = t_bseg.
      ls_recjour_input-wa_bset = t_bset.
      ls_recjour_input-tcode =  sy-tcode .

      concatenate ls_recjour_input-wa_bkpf-belnr ls_recjour_input-wa_bkpf-bukrs ls_recjour_input-wa_bkpf-gjahr into lv_objkey.

      CALL METHOD cl_swf_evt_event=>get_event_container
        EXPORTING
          im_objcateg  = cl_swf_evt_event=>mc_objcateg_cl
          im_objtype   = lv_objtype
          im_event     = lv_event
        RECEIVING
          re_reference = lr_event_parameters.

      TRY.
          CALL METHOD lr_event_parameters->set
            EXPORTING
              name  = lv_param_name
              value = ls_recjour_input.

        CATCH cx_swf_cnt_cont_access_denied .
        CATCH cx_swf_cnt_elem_access_denied .
        CATCH cx_swf_cnt_elem_not_found .
        CATCH cx_swf_cnt_elem_type_conflict .
        CATCH cx_swf_cnt_unit_type_conflict .
        CATCH cx_swf_cnt_elem_def_invalid .
        CATCH cx_swf_cnt_container .
     ENDTRY.

       TRY.
          CALL METHOD cl_swf_evt_event=>raise
            EXPORTING
              im_objcateg        = cl_swf_evt_event=>mc_objcateg_cl
              im_objtype         = lv_objtype
              im_event           = lv_event
              im_objkey          = lv_objkey
              im_event_container = lr_event_parameters.
          COMMIT WORK.
        CATCH cx_swf_evt_invalid_objtype .
        CATCH cx_swf_evt_invalid_event .
      ENDTRY.

      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
*        WRITE: 'Error occurred while triggering workflow event.' ##NO_TEXT.
      ENDIF.
   ENDIF.
   ENDIF.

STEP – 5: Now we need to create tasks in PFTC, for the methods that were created in STEP-3. This is a very normal procedure of creating the tasks that are to be consumed by the activity in the Workflow Template.

NOTE: For the User Decision Step, take a copy of the standard decision step (TS00008267) and do the necessary modifications and perform agent assignment. Once done, capture the Agent Assignment in a Customizing TR by running the report RHMOVE30 in SE38.

 STEP – 6: I had created Flexible Workflow for this requirement. Open the transaction “SWDD_SCENARIO”. Create the Workflow containers with reference to all the event and the method parameters in the Custom Class created in STEP-3. 

  1. In the Process Data Tab, provide the class name and the associated event. Do the necessary event binding and activate the event as shown below.

    NOTE: Caputre the Event Activation in the same customizing TR as mentioned in STEP - 5.
  2. In the Activities Tab, create the activities for the tasks that were created in STEP-5. Include the User Decision Activity, in the list of activities.

    NOTE: “DISPLAY_TCODE” is an activity which we have created for accessing the T-Code “FBD1”.
  3. In the Conditions Tab, we have created one condition. This condition is for stopping the Workflow upon completion of all the agents.
  4. When we create Flexible Workflow, we must create a customized Runtime Class. This Runtime Class helps us to utilize “Instance Methods” upon available events. We need to create a Subclass of the standard Runtime Class “CL_SWF_FLEX_IFS_RUN_APPL_BASE”. Then we can redefine the methods necessary for our purpose. There would be several standard methods which would be useful for several scenarios.
    NOTE: For our process, we can redefine below methods, as shown below.

    NOTE: First method would get triggered just before the Workflow is about to complete. Second method would trigger upon completion of each activity. Use TaskId as filter, such that procedure only for suitable tasks would run.

    Inside the Result Callback method, utilize the mail notification methods created in STEP-3.

  5. Activate the Workflow. Since we are implementing Flexible Workflow, we need to activate the scenario. Follow the path mentioned below.
    SPRO -> ABAP Platform -> Application Server -> Business Management -> SAP Business Workflow -> Flexible Workflow -> Scenario Activation. 

    NOTE: Provide the Custom Workflow Number and activate the scenario. You can capture this in TR & transport to further landscape.

  6. Now we need to Login to the FIORI launchpad. As Pre-requisites implement “Manage Workflows” & “Manage Workflow Scenarios” applications.
    NOTE: In the “Manage Workflows” application create the Workflow, along with the sequence of the steps in which the workflow must be executed.
    Manage Workflow Header:

    Execution Steps:


    Here the maximum level is 4. However, if we keep the maximum approver level less than 4 also, the Workflow will stop at the designated maximum level. This is based the Step-Condition indicated as Final Level in the above screenshot.

    Once created, open the “Manage Workflow Scenarios” application and export the scenario. This will get downloaded as a XML file in the system.

  7. Move the Customizing TR and import the downloaded XML file into the testing landscape and to further landscapes.
  8. Upon Complete approval of the workflow, the “BLOCKED_FOR_POSTING” & “XDELT (Deletion_Indicator) fields would be reset. This makes the document eligible for further posting through Perioidic Job Scheduling in F.14 Transaction.
  9. Incase if the approver rejects the document, he can provide the Reason for Rejection. This will be sent along with the Rejection Notification to the Initiator of the Workflow (i.e Recurring Entry Document Creator). 
  10. The Document Creator can modify according to the Rejection Comments and save the document. The Workflow would get triggered again for another approval cycle.
    NOTE: I had designed the solution such that the document cannot be edited, unless it is fully approved / rejected. This is an optional solution to be incorporated. 

 Conclusion:
Recurring entries simplify financial operations, but without governance, they can introduce compliance risks. By integrating FBD1 with an approval workflow, organizations can achieve the right balance between automation and financial control, ensuring accuracy, transparency, and audit readiness.

entry.title: Re: Error: Failed to get transports for selected package...
entry.content:
Hi, since the package and transport layer are correct and the roles are assigned, the error typically indicates that the backend cannot propose a modifiable Workbench request to Web IDE.

Please verify the following on the ABAP system:
1. Client changeability (SCC4 / SE06)
The client must allow repository changes. Otherwise CTS cannot return any transport request and the deployment fails.
SAP KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/2870168
2. ADT service availability
Ensure the ICF service /sap/bc/adt is active in SICF. Web IDE retrieves transport requests via ADT.
3. Existing open Workbench request
Create a new unreleased Workbench request in SE09/SE10 with the same user and retry the deployment.

In most cases one of these backend conditions prevents the transport proposal.

entry.title: Re: Hi experts, I have a below scenario about posting unp...
entry.content:
• If you want MRP to react immediately when scrap is posted, you’ll need a custom enhancement:
    • Capture scrap quantity at confirmation.
    • Adjust order header quantity via BAPI_PRODORD_CHANGE or a user exit.
  • Be cautious: reducing order quantity changes dependent requirements and may affect cost settlement.
  • Alternatively, keep the order quantity fixed and let MRP react only after GR — this is SAP’s standard design, but less responsive for long‑running orders.

entry.title: Re: Mass update condition records amount
entry.content:
The already suggested SAP Note 3665364 is about mass maintenance of condition records in S/4HANA (especially for custom condition types). It provides guidance on simplifying uploads/updates (e.g., via APIs, CDS views, or mass maintenance transactions) instead of relying on LSMW. It helps with how to update condition records more efficiently, but it does not change the access sequence logic or automatically replicate prices across customers.

Practical options are

Custom Program
Build a custom program to replicate condition records across customers. In some projects, they implement a Z‑program that reads condition records for one customer and writes them for all others.

Condition Maintenance via APIs
In S/4HANA, you can use the Condition Record OData API to automate replication. This is cleaner than LSMW and aligns with SAP’s direction.

Reference Customer Approach
If business rules allow, you can define a “reference customer” for pricing. Other customers inherit pricing from this reference, reducing duplication. But this requires a change in pricing design.

entry.title: Re: From Business Objects to Business Flows: The Missing Layer of RAP Documentation
entry.content:
@r_lindemann Interesting, I will have a look at ARC42 and see if it can fit us. From your description it sounds like what I'm trying to achieve, but without re-inventing the wheel.

@Joery  Unfortunately I cannot open source or share it, but I have built a simple MCP on top of @MarcelloUrbani 's excellent project https://github.com/marcellourbani/abap-adt-api , it seems it should be possible to also use the ABAPFS as a MCP, but I didn't want the dependency on VS Code.


By simply giving a LLM tools to search for objects, read source code and performing where-used I could get the LLM (Claude Sonnet 4.6 through Kiro in my case) to do pretty good class diagrams and UMLs using  plantUML in markdowns. It also did an excellent job in documenting dependencies and find orphaned code.  

I haven't got it to do very good flow charts though, not yet.

entry.title: Can’t Find the Reassign Option? Here’s How to Enable It!
entry.content:
👋Hey Everyone,

Recently, I came across a situation where a customer was unable to find the navigation steps to enable the “Reassign” option. So, I’m sharing the correct steps here for anyone who might face the same issue.

Steps to Enable the Reassign Option:

  1. Go to Setup
  2. Navigate to Workflow / Approval → Workflow
  3. Click Edit based on your quote status (e.g., Preparing → Preparing)
  4. Enable the Reassign checkbox and Save 
  5. Now, go to Reassign Rights
  6. Click Add New
  7. Select the User Type who can reassign the quote and the To User Types based on your requirement
  8. Next, go to Workflow Permissions
  9. Edit the Reassign action and select the required User Type(e.g., Admin)
  10. Don’t forget to Save the changes ✅

After completing these steps, you should be able to see the Reassign option in your quote.

I’ve attached images for better understanding. Hope this helps! 😊

 

Thank you for reading this article 🙂

Stay tuned!!!

entry.title: Managing Print Forms in SAP Logistics Management
entry.content:
Out of the box, SAP Logistics Management (LGM thereafter) comes with pre-delivered print templates, that you can use as-is if they meet your requirements, but you can also create your own custom print templates and tailor them to your specific requirements (maybe you want to add a logo, maybe a parcel carrier you work with has a specific layout/formatting requirement, ...), whatever they may be.

The app 'Manage Print Templates' is your one stop shop for all things related to LGM print templates, whether they are yours or the ones that SAP pre delivers.

All SAP delivered templates follow a specific naming convention and they are easily identifiable as they start with the characters '/SAP/', as shown below.

 Whilst you can use the SAP templates in your own processes/documents (where applicable), you cannot modify them.

If you wish to modify or create your own form templates, then you first have to copy them into your own customer namespace. To do so, locate the print template that will serve as your source (here I will be using an SAP template, since my system is brand new, but you can of course also use one of your existing customer specific templates and build on that). 

As an example, I will use the SAP Handling Unit Shipping Label as my source. I then select the Copy option.

 In the next screen, I will simply amend the name of the print template to give it a Custom co-notation (by very creatively prefixing it with a Z) and also a customer description. I will keep everything else as-is and choose the 'Create' Option to save my print template.

At this point, my custom print template, if I was to reference it as a template to use in my processes it would look exactly like the one delivered by SAP, because I have not modified the form yet per se!

To proceed with this step, I first need to download the template form file (the file that holds the layout - the fields I will print, what font I will use, where they will be placed, etc..). To do this you can click on the form template hyperlink as shown (some browsers offer the ability to 'Save link as').

 

The .xdp file will be downloaded in your usual download folder.

At this point, note that in order to be able to modify the .xdp file, you will need to download a specific software, named Adobe LiveCycle Designer, which you can obtain from the SAP software download center. Also note that this software is only available for the Windows environment.

To download this software go the the SAP Software Center at the URL https://me.sap.com/softwarecenter  , and then search for 'Adobe Livecycle Designer' (referred to as ALD from now).

 Locate, download and install the version as indicated.

You can change the name of the downloaded file (SAP_HU_SHIPPING_LABEL_EN.xdp) later, but I would suggest you make a copy and then change the name (again being creative and naming it like my custom print template - but I could have renamed it with a completely unrelated name too).

 Now fire up ADL, and open the file (Menu  File --> Open) you downloaded and renamed. If you get a message about a missing .xsd file, just click ok to continue. Your form and layout will appear.

The point of this blog is not to go in the details of how to use ALD, but the screenshot below will give you an idea of what you are seeing and what you can do.

 I make my changes to the form (I change the label of the 'Product' field to be 'Product Identification Number', change the formatting to be italic text, and I add an image to represent how the product should be handled - with a forklift). I save my changes and close ADL.

 I now need to go back to LGM and upload my modified .xsd file into my custom print template. In my custom print template, I switch to edit mode and then either click on the input field that has the form name, or click the 'Browse' button to locate my modified form.

Once my modified file is located, I select it, and click open to upload it to LGM. 

 Once the upload is complete, you can save your change.

 Also, in passing, I will mention that you can set the output format to either be pdf (Portable Document Format) or zpl (Zebra Programming Language), depending on your preferred or available output technology.

 

The last thing you need to do is to update the relevant processing rule so that your modified customer template form is referenced and determined (and used !) in the right process. In this case, we modified an Handling Unit Shipping Label, so we will update the PRINT_TEMPLATE_RULESET_FOR_WAREHOUSE_V1 processing rule to reflect this (along with the condition(s) that make the use of this print template true.

Of course you will want to test this, to make sure that the changes you have made print exactly how you designed them to be. Since here we are talking about a handling unit, then if you are not physically printing the label, you will need to go to the app 'Monitor Handling Units', and for your handling unit check the pdf or zpl that was output!

entry.title: The ABAP Detective and the CSV Kraken
entry.content:
Beginning at the end:

OCR version of a paper document, shown in Emacs

I was surprised at the good OCR work here, except for the made-up words that are just dots. I'm a detective, ABAP Squad, now working in the non-profit ways and by-ways around town. One of my recent cases was to unravel and resurface a chronological list of events, possibly including personnel details, excluding personal details as much as possible. I used a few Open Source software tools that work for jobs like this. There are other Excel cleansers, I've just got my ways.

Read this in either the Captain's log cadence from Star Trek, or the Joe Friday Dragnet monotone, if you like.

Detection isn't always finding something hidden or undiscovered. sometimes it's finding something that isn't what it seems, like a misaligned character reference, or a faulty conclusion based on suspect data.

I was recently "gifted" a pack of spreadsheets representing historic events and persons, recent history, not ancient. One captured shows, the other cast and crew. Specific details are not important: think header and detail tables. My assignment to "clean up" the data, an open question being the end state was unstated. Free reign.

Data purification rituals can be done inside spreadsheets, or outside, or a combination of both if the relationships are complex. In this case, merging 2 distinct worksheets seemed easier outside than in, to me. I was not about to try to join the sheets in a worksheet tool, Excel, or LibreOffice. You can substitute the database of your choice in the following workflow steps. I chose PostgreSQL as the free software of the moment.

Other free software I'll mention along the trail, but the crux is the discovery process of converting someone else's worksheets into a more usable entity, basically reverse engineering any "stupid Excel tricks" and idioms.

A quick review of a new worksheet might give a sense of the scope of "normalizing" the content as a database administrator would do to prepare a dictionary or relationship diagram for data cleanup and different perspectives.

In an SAP-centric world, you could build the tables I did in HANA or Sybase or the PostgreSQL I used if allowed, but this is a small and simple set of tables with the main interest being a date range in decades. Y2K will be
part of the tale.

31-DEC-2025 prequel on the python beat
.. 2330 Dec 31 15:59 spot_season_png.py
I doodled with minimal image files based on date ranges, basically showing on/off. I tried to keep it short without adding the PIL and just write a 4x366 PNG file after reading a CSV stream, 70 - 80 lines of Python, good to keep my hand in the syntax, rules, and available libraries (on a range of free OS; Cygwin included).

E.g.

PNG 366 x 4 pixels, red + black.

Red shows event ongoing, black shows none. These 12 months are a first approximation based on initial research.

09-JAN-2026 data delivery

I got 2 Excel files via email, then converted them into LibreOffice for review and analysis. Last edited in 2022, one had a bit of markup where cell text was red-colored, and the other had gaps in the rows between years. Without belaboring the data matrix, there were dates, event titles, people's names, and commentary. Basic worksheet content, with character.

In LibreOffice forms, connected database tables must have a primary key to allow live edits. If you just throw a random worksheet into an un-keyed table, LO gives you read-only access. So step 1 in looking at the content is to determine if a primary key exists, hopefully in 1 or 2 columns, but somewhere. The trial-and-error method is to pick a key/keys and try to load, same as with column widths ("what's the longest last name we allow?").

The sheet had blank rows, or rows with minimal information that I could safely ignore, and the keys looked adequate in that only one event occurred during each month, or so it looked. A first load with year and month as the key actually worked with minimal "air-brushing" of data. The rookie mistake here is to assume the loaded sequential order will persist in the database with later listings. Nope, as soon as one adds or edits rows, the "SELECT" order is unpredictable. Meaning sub-divisions finer than month (day, week, weekend) get out of order without another key. I manually populated counters in the source sheet.

 Column |      Type      | Collation | Nullable | Default
--------+----------------+-----------+----------+---------
 col_a  | character(30)  |           | not null |
[...]
 col_h  | character(12)  |           | not null |
Indexes:
    "show_sheet_raw_pkey" PRIMARY KEY, btree (col_a, col_h)

Event dates are in a US-backwards Month/Year order, part text, part numeric: "Dec 62". And pre-Y2K, we figured 62 was the bomb (atomic, that is, not the database kind).

15-JAN-2026 data ingestion and digestion

I created and filled the first database, using PGSQL 15 server on a Pi Linux kernel 6.12

db=> \copyright
PostgreSQL Database Management System
(also known as Postgres, formerly known as Postgres95)

Dates were a mess, one table using a single column and the other 2. I definitely wanted these as actual data types, and at some point, spanned from a single date point to a range ("run/event duration").

Enter a lookup table, inline.

create view v_shows as (
  select
    case
       when  substr(col_a,1,3) = 'Jan'  then  1
       when  substr(col_a,1,3) = 'Feb'  then  2
       when  substr(col_a,1,3) = 'Mar'  then  3
       when  substr(col_a,1,3) = 'Apr'  then  4
       when  substr(col_a,1,3) = 'May'  then  5
       when  substr(col_a,1,3) = 'Jun'  then  6
       when  substr(col_a,1,4) = 'June' then  6
       when  substr(col_a,1,3) = 'Jul'  then  7
       when  substr(col_a,1,4) = 'July' then  7
[...]

Probably slicker ways to do this; I originally didn't spot the 4-character months amidst the 3-character ones. I use a "raw" style to slurp in CSV files to the database, then either build a view or copy the values to a second table, or both. The raw table just has characters sized to fit the feed, and then date and other types are added as needed.

Here, I needed date math; searching found this usable (as of 2026):

stackoverflow.com/questions/10170544/getting-results-between-two-dates-in-postgresql 

21-JAN-2026 enumeration

In one column, characteristics were overlaid, leaving a normalization challenge to split cast from crew, and also them from band. I needed to infer which was which by content, where "JOHN / BILL" means John acts as Bill, but "JOHN / PIANO" means John's in the band. More on this later; meanwhile I built in a switch/case for roles. One can do more than one, which likely means more than one row as further refinement.

create type cast_or_crew as enum (
  'band',
  'cast',
  'crew',
  'n/a',
  'null'
)
;

create table role (
  role  cast_or_crew
)
;

Later I modified this to be "band_or_cast_or_crew".

ERROR: null value in column "col_a" of relation "show_sheet_raw" violates not-null constraint

22-JAN-2026 double quotes acting as ditto marks

A few rows had "double-quotes", or ditto-marks in the vernacular, showing the prior data was to be repeated. This goes back to manual/electric typewriters (not to mention ditto paper and machines). The workaround I needed was for cases where the original source had double quotes, whether in the title, or a manual combination of 2 elements double-headed.

where
  col_b not like '%The Typists%'
and (
    col_b like '%"%"%'
  or
    col_c like '%"%"%'
)
;

double hidden elisions elipsis -- ASCII or Unicode?

With the base assumption that the content to be processed was likely ASCII, I could treat the CSV file to be imported as 1 byte, 7 bits for each character. Accent marks were possible, and the odd non-character might have crept in over the millennia of scribes. I did the quick approach and didn't look in detail until later. Turns out this suspicion was founded, and there were no backwards/forwards double quotes. Only the dot-dot-dot that Excel likely converted to a 3 byte UTF-8 string:

12	82	Starting Here…	Charles Peggy 	
12	82	Starting Here…	Charles Tom

Easy to miss on eye-checks; …

84 | One Flew Over The? | Mattheau

Those characters, whether left in or omitted during data loads, need to be backfilled with the correct information, presumably truncated to save time, and fit a desired width.

NULL ness

08-FEB-2026 final stretches

The final touches were to address the metadata / color encoding somehow. The volunteers who shared the historic event data used a shorthand way of specifying either additional verification was needed, or there were known knowledge gaps. An asterisk as a flag field worked great in a database sense, red-letter days not so much. An example illustration of the way I used:

Color Filter menu path - LibreOffice

The challenge is recognizing what content is flagged, and storing that in a column with a known type, possibly linking to another table or having a constraint. I searched for tricks to parse worksheets based on cell text color and found it much more difficult than expected ("Here's a 50 line Windows Power Shell script from the random-net", er, no thanks).

The LibreOffice trick (which may differ in Excel) works in this case as the row count is mercifully small. Much more than a few hundred rows makes this trickier to get right. Set AutoFilter on the column headings, maybe not the first row. Then click the pull-down icon, choose "Filter by Color", and then the spectrum appears, in this case red, black and um Automatic.

NULL matters!

After processing the flags, I needed to check they registered. Almost missed the NULL logic but knew something was amiss when the count was short. Empty is not the same as NULL!

db=> select color_code, count(color_code) from v_shows group by color_code;
                 color_code                 | count
--------------------------------------------+-------
                                            |     0
 RED                                        |   110
(2 rows)

db=> select count(*) from v_shows where color_code is NULL;
 count
-------
   349
(1 row)

The view above also gives a glimpse at yet another quality control data cleanup approach, showing the ditto marks, empty rows, extra quotes, and a leading space in Medea. That last needed to be manually bumped back into place so later queries could sort and join correctly.

final stretches spelling counts

Spelling errors can creep in by typos, or other means, or words might be names or deliberate misspellings. I'm good at database detecting but I also have a utility belt of tools to speed the work. Plain old UNIX command line sort and spell are indispensable.

$ head -7  show_roles_crew_bad_spell.txt
Asst
Choreographerr
Construntion
Coordinator
Debrah
Deirector
Deisgn

 Case by case basis here, where "Asst" is perfectly acceptable (dropping the last character would not be, but spell check would not see that). "Debrah" I recognize as a person's name, spelled however they chose. Any they could change their billing over time, say adding a middle initial to differentiate or whatever. Note to self, add another table called AKA.

final stretches: CSV + PNG

What goes in comes out we hope. I wanted to see two outputs/outcomes; first an event list in chronological order, and then, an infographic based on that. Other uses will flow from these, but it made sense to set simple requirements and put blinders on to other side cases.

\copy ( select season, opening, closing, show  from show_more order by opening, row_id) to /tmp/shows.csv csv header ;

 

20-FEB-2026 next steps

Besides the obvious gaps and red flags, is this case solved? I was able to generate info-graphics, fix spelling and other data entry errors, and turn around the cleansed content in a CSV format for return to other historians.

I'd like to generate event summaries and details in a web format, which leads to the OCR and Unicode character saga. For more reference material, I scanned a document on Linux with XSane, then ran it through the scripting known as "OCR my PDF", as illustrated at the start of this post.

I wanted to put a "red dot" between strings, so a digest could be output instead of a long streamer.  Which led to experimenting using Python and beyond ASCII content. I could query the database, then write a text file, similar to the CSV above.

    for row in records_1:
        print(":", row[0] , row[1], row[2] )
        # text = text + row[0] + ' \u2981 '
        text = text + row[0] + ' 🔴 '

I then learned about what I now call "upper and lower case" Unicode through Python syntax on Mastodon.

12-FEB-2026 : https://chaos.social/@jspath55/116058419584370137

"Could not show this in #Python with #Unicode '\u' escape codes and ended up pasting it from https://www.compart.com/en/unicode/U+1F534"

(later comment):

"\U0001F534 Unfortunate decision, but it was made back in the 90s after Unicode was extended beyond the Basic Multilingual Plane."

entry.title: Re: Bank Statement Reconciliation
entry.content: Hello, Using the app 'manage Bank Statement' you can post the statement items in the system. Just by posting the items, system will not to auto-clearing of corresponding open items if there is no 'Processing Rule' in place. Open supplier item and open statement items just sit without shaking hands. In the processing rule we actually tell the system the 'logic' to find each other. Of course you can automate these rules so that auto clearing goes through. When you reprocess the statement items using the app 'Rprocess bank statement item', system prompts you to convert this 'reprocessing' into a rule. Refer to this link for details.

entry.title: Change History Log of Debug Mode SE16H for Audit
entry.content:
Hi All SAP Experts.
currently there is an inquiry from the key-user about whether the requirement of data changes log for audit trail in SAP system that have been updated/deleted using debug mode /H particularly is feasible via transaction code SE16N only?

or perhaps could we also have change history log upon the usage of transaction code SE16H with debug mode? because there is such an audit findings regarding data update/deletion from table done via transaction code SE16H with debug mode, but somehow the changes log or history could not be traced nor be captured. Instead, data update/deletion via SE16N with debug mode could be tracked or recorded into standard table SE16N_CD_KEY.

if debug mode is also possible to be used for transaction code SE16H, then should audit use any other standard table rather than SE16N_CD_KEY to get the data changes log?

Thank you so much, kindly help us on giving advice and we are looking forward to discussing this further with you.

Ani.

cc @LEE325 @RaymondGiuseppi @manuf 

SAP Audit Management logging of SAP GUI for Windows SAP Access Control for SAP S/4HANA SAP Financial Information Management SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database SAP IT Operations Analytics SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: RBP Access
entry.content:
Hi @bbknrt,

I recommend exploring the RBP Troubleshooting tool. It provides clear insights into a user's permissions, the permission roles granting them, and enables side-by-side comparisons between users, along with other valuable features. This could at least help understanding why the other user doesn't have access to the transaction while you have.

Full details are available at https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/using-role-based-permissions/role-based-permissions-troubleshooting-tool?locale=en-US&version=LATEST.

Best, 
Fabian

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI ODATA V2 jaon data POST request Issue
entry.content: i have a OData v2 standard service giving same error with json paylaod. """" Request Payload Parsing Failed for one of the reason: (1) Either the OData metadata content available in the server is outdated OR (2) The request payload seems to be incorrect. Error Details : only whitespace content allowed before start tag and not { (position: START_DOCUMENT seen {... @1:1) , org.xmlpull.v1.XmlPullParserException: only whitespace content allowed before start tag and not { (position: START_DOCUMENT seen {... @1:1) """". so i thought the same its due to JSON format. but i then tried to implement HTTP adpater in my iflow and using same JSON payload. well it works with HTTP call. so i doubt its format issue. i have faced many time such error with OData adapter during POST "" whitespace ....etc """. If anyone can please point out what is the root cause of this issue?. According to my research on OData(POST) call. {While creating an entity, if a property is not present in the payload then a null value is passed to the OData server.} from this i think that somehow blank payload is being sent to adapter, but dont know why. Thanks in advance for anyone who would be taking up to resolve this.

entry.title: Re: BAPI_SHIPMENT_CHANGE clean core alternative?
entry.content:
It is really a strategic decision.
There will not be released options for LE-TRA and it is unlikely that they will come in the future like @Dominik_Tylczynski indicated.
That leads to the conclusion that there is no real clean core option here.

In that case it is just a decision whether to use the known BAPI, thus leave it as is.
Alternatively you could make a wrapper and release that one, in my opinion this does not make it better for your situation.

Yes in general RAP, API, classes take priority.
But if there is currently no standard alternatives towards clean core there is nothing we can do in these situations other than keeping a list of these things and check post upgrades if there is an alternative in the newer release.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One (SQL) – Unique Users with Assigned L...
entry.content:
1. Is there any SQL table where license details are stored? - NO

  2. Can license details be extracted directly through a query? - NO

  3. Or is this information available only via License Administration / License Server? - NO

  4. What is the recommended approach to get a unique user-wise license list? - EXCEL

Excel file with license descriptions and formulas to determine their availability and allocation according to the database.
Take the license description from the license allocation window and create a sheet with rows for users and license type, and columns for the databases where you create that user and grant access.

Note that assigning licenses to a demo database and then deleting the database without removing the license from the user will result in license loss, which can only be recovered by re-importing the license file.

entry.title: Re: how to change Gl accounts for journal entry type
entry.content:
Good day @jerrysam 

Thank you for your question.

You can align the company code 33xx with company code 10xx, but only through the available public cloud configurations, not through classic SPRO customising which is restricted. The public cloud typically handles this by checking and adjusting:

  • Automatic Account Determination for the G/L account. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/89f2a87accc849d4b5682feb619ce9ad.html  and https://help.sap.com/docs/s4hana-best-practices/ycoa/account-determination for more details.
  • Functional area logic, which is often from G/L master, cost element context, substitution and default assignment logic depending on your setup.

If company code 10xx and 33xx post to different functional areas for the same CBXN scenario, this can often be linked to one or more of these differences:

  • Different account determination configuration
  • Different sales org, chart of accounts, and account assignment group combination
  • Different G/L master settings
  • Different functional area setup
  • Different master data used by the posting.

Also, CBXN is a free goods related item category used in S4H Cloud sales scenarios, so the item category itself can be part of the context, but the final FI posting is still driven by your configuration and derivation logic.

This blog provides some insights: Item category determination in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

A recommended approach is:

  • Before changing anything, compare a posted example from 10xx and 33xx in both company codes first. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/a570b6535fe6b74ce10000000a174cb4.html for more details.
  • In your billing 33xx document, identify which account key was used, which fields were used for determination and which account 51301xxxx was selected instead of 51001xxx
  • Check the relevant account determination setting in your PC configuration are. Compare chart of accounts, sales org, customer account assignment group, material account assignment group, etc.
  • Functional area may not always come from the same point as the G/L account. Even if you align G/L account, you still need to verify why 606x is used in 33xx while 60xx is used in 10xx.

Question: what happens if you change the settings?

Answer: future posting for the same key combination will use the new G/L account or logic. New billing documents will follow the updated logic. Documents you already posted will not be changed automatically, sales scenarios that use the same key combination might be impacted. Therefore, test in Q system first with the same CBXN example before moving to production.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: PR not visible under purchase data tab in CJ20N
entry.content:
Key checks you need to make

Material Master Settings
Ensure the material has a valid procurement type (F, E, or X) and MRP views maintained. If the material is project stock relevant, check that the special stock indicator (Q) is set correctly.

Project/Network Settings
In CJ20N, confirm that the network activity is released and has the correct control key (procurement allowed). The “Res./Purc. immediately” flag should trigger PR creation, but only if the network is released and dates are valid.

MRP Controller / Plant Assignment
The material must be assigned to the correct plant and MRP controller. If the plant is missing or inconsistent, PR creation will fail silently.

Availability of Procurement Parameters
Check transaction OPSN (parameters for network procurement). Ensure the settings allow automatic PR creation for your network type.

Authorization
Sometimes PR creation fails due to missing authorization for the user to create PRs in the background.

Log / Error Messages
Run CJ20N → Edit → Generate Purchase Requisition manually and check the log. If it says “0 entries were changed,” it means the system did not find eligible components (often due to missing procurement type or incorrect assignment).

entry.title: Joule Studio: Leveraging Agents, Joule Skills, and Document Grounding to extend your use case
entry.content:
Joule Studio: Leveraging Agents, Joule Skills, and Document Grounding to extend your use case

Pre-requisites:

  • Joule Studio
  • Joule Base
  • For document grounding: AI Core (extended plan), AI Launchpad, Object Store

Joule Studio, part of SAP Build, enables developers to extend Joule with Agent Builder and Skills using a low-code/no-code. It provides seamless integration with SAP and Non-SAP data.

With Joule Studio, I created custom agent such that users can simply ask Joule to create a service order whenever an issue arises. Based on the instructions, Joule automatically gather the details, classify the issue based on the company policy using document grounding and create the order in S/4 HANA Public Cloud.

In this blog post, we’ll explore how you can take Joule further with Joule Studio by integrating agents, skills, and document grounding that make your application smarter and more dynamic.

 

1. Setup

Agent Builder

Ensure that you have AI Agent Builder activated. You'll need to toggle the 

 

Document Grounding

This is an amazing blog written by Daniel on setting up Document Grounding in Joule Studio: link

Make sure the extensibility_user role is assigned to your account to access the document grounding feature in Joule Studio.

When new files are added to the S3 folder, you can trigger a manual sychronisation to refresh and ground the documents.

 

Application Setup

To connect your application data to Joule Studio, you can either set up an MCP server or use the BTP Destination Service, depending on your integration needs.

Both options provide seamless connectivity, but the choice depends on the specific use case and the systems you're integrating with Joule Studio.

In this project, I’m using destination service.

In BTP Destination Service, ensure that you include the additional properties listed for it to appear in Joule Studio:

  • sap.processautomation.enabled: true
  • sap.build.usage: odata.gen
  • sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled: true

 

2. Use Actions Project in Joule Studio to encapsulate the API actions

Once the BTP Destination is setup, it will appear in the OData Destination and you can create the Actions Project.

Once the actions project is setup, release and publish it. 

 

3. Create new Joule Skill Project

Within a project, you can setup multiple skills and agents.

Skills are predefined, deterministic workflows that execute single, atomic operations with known inputs and outputs. They function as rule-based tasks and perform repetitive operations without requiring reasoning.  For example, a skill in this project is to get information based on a service order ID.  

Agents are autonomous, multi-tool systems that solve complex problems through dynamic reasoning, planning, and execution. Agents leverage large language models for reasoning, decision-making, and planning, while dynamically selecting and executing the right tools based on the specific context.

 

How Skills and Agents Work Together

Agent Planning: The agent analyzes the user request and determines which skills or tools are needed to accomplish the task.

Skill Execution: The agent invokes relevant Joule skills to perform specific operations like data retrieval or process execution.

Result: The agent combines outputs from multiple skills to provide comprehensive solutions and recommendations

 

Create a Skill

Provide a clear and detailed description of the scenario you want the skill to handle – the more specific you are, the better the outcome will be.

Add “Call Action” to pass the necessary fields to the API to trigger the process.

Add the variables to be passed to the API.

In my project, I have created multiple skills and added them as tools to my agent.

Demo sample:

 

Conclusion

In conclusion, extending Joule Studio to connect with your application data through the MCP server, BTP Destination Service  or API connections, enables seamless integration with external systems. By developing Joule skills and agents, leveraging document grounding and other tools, you can enhance Joule's capabilities and integrate it with your applications.

 

Check out the following resources to help you get started:
Activate Joule Studio

Developer’s Tutorial: Use Joule Studio to Create an Agent

Document Grounding in SAP Joule Studio
